│ │ Recent activity │

 │ Welcome back Steven! │ No recent activity │

 │ │ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │

 │ ▐ ▛███▜ ▌ │ What's new │

 │ ▝▜ █████ ▛▘ │ Added `path` parameter to the `EnterWorktree` tool to switch into an existing worktree of the current repository │

 │ ▘▘ ▝▝ │ Added PreCompact hook support: hooks can now block compaction by exiting with code 2 or returning `{"decision":"block"}` │

 │ Opus 4.6 (1M context) with hi… · Claude Max · │ Added background monitor support for plugins via a top-level `monitors` manifest key that auto-arms at session start or on skill invoke │

 ● high · /effort

 ● high · /effort

 ❯ Read CLAUDE.md Current State and ~/.claude/plans/spicy-sleeping-gadget.md in full,
 then continue Session 61 from the exact next step stated in CLAUDE.md.

 Session 60 ended mid-execution at context limit. Two things shipped (schedule ID

 map fix in commit 846aaed, session wrap docs in commit 616e184). The research

 engine Round 1 plan is approved but implementation has NOT started.

 Start at Commit 1 — Part 0 shared dedup fix. Execute in this order:

 1. Write scripts/test_round1_unit.py with 7 failing TDD tests covering the

 _merge_contact_upgrade helper (test cases listed in the plan file under

 "Part 0 test cases").

 2. Implement _merge_contact_upgrade(existing: dict, new: dict) -> None in

 tools/contact_extractor.py with the upgrade-on-collision rule specified

 in the plan (fill email if missing, upgrade confidence VERIFIED > LIKELY

 > INFERRED > UNKNOWN, fill empty title, never drop, never downgrade).

 3. Rewrite extract_from_multiple (tools/contact_extractor.py:214-235) to use

 the helper.

 4. Rewrite ResearchJob._merge_contacts (tools/research_engine.py:1635-1649) to

 import and use the helper.

 5. Run .venv/bin/python scripts/test_round1_unit.py — must be 7/7 green.

 6. Run the Level 2 pre-merge integration check: stash changes, run

 ResearchJob("Waverly School District 145", "NE").run() as baseline, save

 result JSON, unstash, rerun, diff. Expected: post.contacts_verified

 >= pre.contacts_verified. ~14 min wall clock, ~$1.50 real API cost.

 7. Commit as: fix(research): upgrade-on-collision in shared dedup logic

 (recovers silently-lost VERIFIED emails)

 Then continue to Commit 2 (Part 1 feature flags) and Commit 3 (Part 2 A/B

 harness), then Level 3 A/B validation run against all 5 locked test targets.

 Remember the load-bearing Session 60 lessons from CLAUDE.md:

 - Don't default to diocesan/charter/CTE examples (public districts are the lane)

 - Budget is $25/week max (not higher)

 - Feature flags, not subclass

 - threading.local() doesn't cross run_in_executor thread boundaries; use

 module globals under CPython GIL + ResearchQueue serial guarantee

 - response.usage token counts replace formula-based cost estimates

 211 return []
 214 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
 215 + # Shared dedup upgrade helper (Round 1 Part 0)
 216 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
 217 + # When two pages return the same person, the old logic silently dropped the
 218 + # second copy. If page 1 had "John Smith no email" and page 2 had "John Smith
 219 + # + VERIFIED email", the VERIFIED version was lost. This helper implements
 220 + # upgrade-on-collision: new info is strictly additive, never downgrading.

 221 +

 222 + _EMAIL_CONFIDENCE_RANK = { "UNKNOWN" : 0 , "INFERRED" : 1 , "LIKELY" : 2 , "VERIFIED" : 3 }

 223 +

 224 +

 225 + def _merge_contact_upgrade ( existing: dict , new: dict ) -> None :

 226 + """

 227 + Upgrade `existing` in place using any richer fields from `new`.

 228 +

 229 + Rules (evaluated in order):

 230 + 1. If new has an email and existing has none → copy email + confidence + source_url

 231 + 2. Elif new.email_confidence outranks existing.email_confidence → upgrade email + confidence + source_url

 232 + 3. If new has a non-empty title and existing has empty title → copy title

 233 + 4. Otherwise leave existing alone

 234 +

 235 + Never drops, never downgrades. Identity fields (first_name, last_name,

 236 + district_name) are not touched — the caller owns the dedup key.

 237 + """

 238 + new_email = (new.get( "email" ) or "" ).strip()

 239 + existing_email = (existing.get( "email" ) or "" ).strip()

 240 + new_conf = (new.get( "email_confidence" ) or "UNKNOWN" ).upper()

 241 + existing_conf = (existing.get( "email_confidence" ) or "UNKNOWN" ).upper()

 242 + new_rank = _EMAIL_CONFIDENCE_RANK.get(new_conf, 0 )

 243 + existing_rank = _EMAIL_CONFIDENCE_RANK.get(existing_conf, 0 )

 244 +

 245 + if new_email and not existing_email:

 246 + existing[ "email" ] = new_email

 247 + existing[ "email_confidence" ] = new_conf

 248 + if new.get( "source_url" ):

 249 + existing[ "source_url" ] = new[ "source_url" ]

 250 + elif new_email and new_rank > existing_rank:

 251 + existing[ "email" ] = new_email

 252 + existing[ "email_confidence" ] = new_conf

 253 + if new.get( "source_url" ):

 254 + existing[ "source_url" ] = new[ "source_url" ]

 255 +

 256 + new_title = (new.get( "title" ) or "" ).strip()

 257 + existing_title = (existing.get( "title" ) or "" ).strip()

 258 + if new_title and not existing_title:

 259 + existing[ "title" ] = new_title

 260 +

 261 +

 262 def extract_from_multiple ( pages: list [ tuple [ str , str ]], district_name: str ) -> list [ dict ]:

 263 """

 264 Extract contacts from multiple (url, content) pairs.

 217 - Deduplicates by (first_name, last_name, district_name) .

 218 - Returns merged, deduped list.

 265 + Deduplicates by (first_name, last_name, district_name) and upgrades

 266 + existing entries on collision so richer info from later pages wins.

 267 """

 220 - all_contacts = []

 221 - seen = set()

 268 + all_contacts : list [ dict ] = []

 269 + index: dict [ tuple [ str , str , str ], dict ] = {}

 271 for url, content in pages:

 272 contacts = extract_contacts(content, url, district_name)

 ...

 274 key = (

 275 c[ "first_name" ].lower(),

 276 c[ "last_name" ].lower(),

 229 - c["district_name"].lower()

 277 + c[ "district_name" ].lower() ,

 278 )

 231 - if key not in seen:

 232 - seen.add(key)

 279 + existing = index.get(key)

 280 + if existing is None :

 281 + index[key] = c

 282 all_contacts.append(c)

 283 + else :

 284 + _merge_contact_upgrade(existing, c)

 286 return all_contacts

 211 return []

 214 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 215 + # Shared dedup upgrade helper (Round 1 Part 0)

 216 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 217 + # When two pages return the same person, the old logic silently dropped the

 218 + # second copy. If page 1 had "John Smith no email" and page 2 had "John Smith

 219 + # + VERIFIED email", the VERIFIED version was lost. This helper implements

 220 + # upgrade-on-collision: new info is strictly additive, never downgrading.

 221 +

 222 + _EMAIL_CONFIDENCE_RANK = { "UNKNOWN" : 0 , "INFERRED" : 1 , "LIKELY" : 2 , "VERIFIED" : 3 }

 223 +

 224 +

 225 + def _merge_contact_upgrade ( existing: dict , new: dict ) -> None :

 226 + """

 227 + Upgrade `existing` in place using any richer fields from `new`.

 228 +

 229 + Rules (evaluated in order):

 230 + 1. If new has an email and existing has none → copy email + confidence + source_url

 231 + 2. Elif new.email_confidence outranks existing.email_confidence → upgrade email + confidence + source_url

 232 + 3. If new has a non-empty title and existing has empty title → copy title

 233 + 4. Otherwise leave existing alone

 234 +

 235 + Never drops, never downgrades. Identity fields (first_name, last_name,

 236 + district_name) are not touched — the caller owns the dedup key.

 237 + """

 238 + new_email = (new.get( "email" ) or "" ).strip()

 239 + existing_email = (existing.get( "email" ) or "" ).strip()

 240 + new_conf = (new.get( "email_confidence" ) or "UNKNOWN" ).upper()

 241 + existing_conf = (existing.get( "email_confidence" ) or "UNKNOWN" ).upper()

 242 + new_rank = _EMAIL_CONFIDENCE_RANK.get(new_conf, 0 )

 243 + existing_rank = _EMAIL_CONFIDENCE_RANK.get(existing_conf, 0 )

 244 +

 245 + if new_email and not existing_email:

 246 + existing[ "email" ] = new_email

 247 + existing[ "email_confidence" ] = new_conf

 248 + if new.get( "source_url" ):

 249 + existing[ "source_url" ] = new[ "source_url" ]

 250 + elif new_email and new_rank > existing_rank:

 251 + existing[ "email" ] = new_email

 252 + existing[ "email_confidence" ] = new_conf

 253 + if new.get( "source_url" ):

 254 + existing[ "source_url" ] = new[ "source_url" ]

 255 +

 256 + new_title = (new.get( "title" ) or "" ).strip()

 257 + existing_title = (existing.get( "title" ) or "" ).strip()

 258 + if new_title and not existing_title:

 259 + existing[ "title" ] = new_title

 260 +

 261 +

 263 """

 264 Extract contacts from multiple (url, content) pairs.

 217 - Deduplicates by (first_name, last_name, district_name) .

 218 - Returns merged, deduped list.

 265 + Deduplicates by (first_name, last_name, district_name) and upgrades

 266 + existing entries on collision so richer info from later pages wins.

 267 """

 271 for url, content in pages:

 ...

 275 c[ "first_name" ].lower(),

 230 + 1. If new has an email and existing has none → copy email + confidence + source_url

 231 + 2. Elif new.email_confidence outranks existing.email_confidence → upgrade email + confidence + source_url

 232 + 3. If new has a non-empty title and existing has empty title → copy title

 211 return []

 214 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 215 + # Shared dedup upgrade helper (Round 1 Part 0)

 216 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 217 + # When two pages return the same person, the old logic silently dropped the

 218 + # second copy. If page 1 had "John Smith no email" and page 2 had "John Smith

 219 + # + VERIFIED email", the VERIFIED version was lost. This helper implements

 220 + # upgrade-on-collision: new info is strictly additive, never downgrading.

 221 +

 222 + _EMAIL_CONFIDENCE_RANK = { "UNKNOWN" : 0 , "INFERRED" : 1 , "LIKELY" : 2 , "VERIFIED" : 3 }

 223 +

 224 +

 225 + def _merge_contact_upgrade ( existing: dict , new: dict ) -> None :

 226 + """

 227 + Upgrade `existing` in place using any richer fields from `new`.

 228 +

 229 + Rules (evaluated in order):

 230 + 1. If new has an email and existing has none → copy email + confidence + source_url

 231 + 2. Elif new.email_confidence outranks existing.email_confidence → upgrade email + confidence + source_url

 232 + 3. If new has a non-empty title and existing has empty title → copy title

 233 + 4. Otherwise leave existing alone

 234 +

 235 + Never drops, never downgrades. Identity fields (first_name, last_name,

 236 + district_name) are not touched — the caller owns the dedup key.

 237 + """

 238 + new_email = (new.get( "email" ) or "" ).strip()

 239 + existing_email = (existing.get( "email" ) or "" ).strip()

 240 + new_conf = (new.get( "email_confidence" ) or "UNKNOWN" ).upper()

 241 + existing_conf = (existing.get( "email_confidence" ) or "UNKNOWN" ).upper()

 242 + new_rank = _EMAIL_CONFIDENCE_RANK.get(new_conf, 0 )

 243 + existing_rank = _EMAIL_CONFIDENCE_RANK.get(existing_conf, 0 )

 244 +

 245 + if new_email and not existing_email:

 246 + existing[ "email" ] = new_email

 247 + existing[ "email_confidence" ] = new_conf

 248 + if new.get( "source_url" ):

 249 + existing[ "source_url" ] = new[ "source_url" ]

 250 + elif new_email and new_rank > existing_rank:

 251 + existing[ "email" ] = new_email

 252 + existing[ "email_confidence" ] = new_conf

 253 + if new.get( "source_url" ):

 254 + existing[ "source_url" ] = new[ "source_url" ]

 255 +

 256 + new_title = (new.get( "title" ) or "" ).strip()

 257 + existing_title = (existing.get( "title" ) or "" ).strip()

 258 + if new_title and not existing_title:

 259 + existing[ "title" ] = new_title

 260 +

 261 +

 263 """

 264 Extract contacts from multiple (url, content) pairs.

 217 - Deduplicates by (first_name, last_name, district_name) .

 218 - Returns merged, deduped list.

 265 + Deduplicates by (first_name, last_name, district_name) and upgrades

 266 + existing entries on collision so richer info from later pages wins.

 267 """

 220 - all_contacts = []

 221 - seen = set()

 268 + all_contacts : list [ dict ] = []

 269 + index: dict [ tuple [ str , str , str ], dict ] = {}

 271 for url, content in pages:

 272 contacts = extract_contacts(content, url, district_name)

 ...

 274 key = (

 275 c[ "first_name" ].lower(),

 276 c[ "last_name" ].lower(),

 229 - c["district_name"].lower()

 277 + c[ "district_name" ].lower() ,

 278 )

 231 - if key not in seen:

 232 - seen.add(key)

 279 + existing = index.get(key)

 280 + if existing is None :

 281 + index[key] = c

 282 all_contacts.append(c)

 283 + else :

 284 + _merge_contact_upgrade(existing, c)

 286 return all_contacts

 676 + # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup. Lowercase + strip trailing slash.

 19 # Module-level cache: district_key → formatted known-contacts string

 22 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
 23 + # Round 1 Flag C: Claude API usage capture
 24 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
 25 + # Captures real input/output token counts from response.usage for A/B cost
 26 + # analysis. Module-level state is safe because:
 27 + # (a) CPython GIL makes list.append atomic, so cross-thread writes from
 28 + # run_in_executor workers don't corrupt the buffer

 29 + # (b) ResearchQueue processes jobs strictly one at a time, so there's no

 30 + # cross-job contamination of the flag or buffer

 31 + # Caller owns start/stop balance via try/finally so the flag never leaks.

 33 + _capture_usage_enabled: bool = False

 34 + _captured_usage: list [ dict ] = []

 35 +

 36 +

 37 + def start_usage_capture () -> None :

 38 + """Begin capturing Claude API usage. Pair with stop_usage_capture in a finally block."""

 39 + global _capture_usage_enabled, _captured_usage

 40 + _capture_usage_enabled = True

 41 + _captured_usage = []

 42 +

 43 +

 44 + def stop_usage_capture () -> list [ dict ]:

 45 + """Stop capture and return accumulated records. Safe to call multiple times."""

 46 + global _capture_usage_enabled

 47 + _capture_usage_enabled = False

 48 + records = list (_captured_usage)

 49 + _captured_usage.clear()

 50 + return records

 51 +

 52 +

 53 + def _log_usage ( response, source_url: str , model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6" ) -> None :

 54 + """Append one Claude call's token usage to the capture buffer if enabled. No-op otherwise."""

 55 + if not _capture_usage_enabled:

 56 + return

 57 + try :

 58 + _captured_usage.append({

 59 + "source_url" : source_url,

 60 + "input_tokens" : response.usage.input_tokens,

 61 + "output_tokens" : response.usage.output_tokens,

 62 + "model" : model,

 63 + })

 64 + except AttributeError:

 65 + pass

 66 +

 67 +

 676 + # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup. Lowercase + strip trailing slash.

 677 + # Catches the common L1/L2/L11/L16/L17 case where the same staff

 678 + # directory URL gets appended multiple times and would otherwise be

 679 + # sent to Claude 2-3x. Off by default — current v1 behavior unchanged.

 680 + if self.enable_url_dedup:

 681 + seen_urls: set [ str ] = set ()

 682 + deduped: list [ tuple [ str , str ]] = []

 683 + for url, content in self.raw_pages:

 684 + normalized = (url or "" ).rstrip( "/" ).lower()

 685 + if normalized and normalized in seen_urls:

 686 + continue

 687 + seen_urls.add(normalized)

 688 + deduped.append((url, content))

 41 + _captured_usage = []

 43 +

 46 + global _capture_usage_enabled

 48 + records = list (_captured_usage)

 49 + _captured_usage.clear()

 50 + return records

 676 + # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup. Lowercase + strip trailing slash.

 677 + # Catches the common L1/L2/L11/L16/L17 case where the same staff

 678 + # directory URL gets appended multiple times and would otherwise be

 679 + # sent to Claude 2-3x. Off by default — current v1 behavior unchanged.

 680 + if self.enable_url_dedup:

 681 + seen_urls: set [ str ] = set ()

 682 + deduped: list [ tuple [ str , str ]] = []

 683 + for url, content in self.raw_pages:

 684 + normalized = (url or "" ).rstrip( "/" ).lower()

 685 + if normalized and normalized in seen_urls:

 686 + continue

 687 + seen_urls.add(normalized)

 688 + deduped.append((url, content))

 689 + before = len (self.raw_pages)

 19 # Module-level cache: district_key → formatted known-contacts string

 20 _known_contacts_cache: dict [ str , str ] = {}

 22 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 23 + # Round 1 Flag C: Claude API usage capture

 24 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 25 + # Captures real input/output token counts from response.usage for A/B cost

 26 + # analysis. Module-level state is safe because:

 27 + # (a) CPython GIL makes list.append atomic, so cross-thread writes from

 28 + # run_in_executor workers don't corrupt the buffer

 29 + # (b) ResearchQueue processes jobs strictly one at a time, so there's no

 30 + # cross-job contamination of the flag or buffer

 31 + # Caller owns start/stop balance via try/finally so the flag never leaks.

 33 + _capture_usage_enabled: bool = False

 34 + _captured_usage: list [ dict ] = []

 35 +

 36 +

 37 + def start_usage_capture () -> None :

 38 + """Begin capturing Claude API usage. Pair with stop_usage_capture in a finally block."""

 39 + global _capture_usage_enabled, _captured_usage

 40 + _capture_usage_enabled = True

 41 + _captured_usage = []

 42 +

 43 +

 44 + def stop_usage_capture () -> list [ dict ]:

 45 + """Stop capture and return accumulated records. Safe to call multiple times."""

 46 + global _capture_usage_enabled

 47 + _capture_usage_enabled = False

 48 + records = list (_captured_usage)

 49 + _captured_usage.clear()

 50 + return records

 51 +

 52 +

 53 + def _log_usage ( response, source_url: str , model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6" ) -> None :

 54 + """Append one Claude call's token usage to the capture buffer if enabled. No-op otherwise."""

 55 + if not _capture_usage_enabled:

 56 + return

 57 + try :

 58 + _captured_usage.append({

 59 + "source_url" : source_url,

 60 + "input_tokens" : response.usage.input_tokens,

 61 + "output_tokens" : response.usage.output_tokens,

 62 + "model" : model,

 63 + })

 64 + except AttributeError:

 65 + pass

 66 +

 67 +

 68 def _get_known_contacts_for_district ( district_name: str ) -> str :

 69 """Load existing contacts for district from Leads tab. Cached per module lifetime."""

 70 if not district_name:

 136 + # Part 1 Flag A: URL dedup in L9

 139 +
 140 + def _fresh_job ( **kwargs ) -> ResearchJob:
 141 + return ResearchJob( "Test ISD" , "TX" , **kwargs)

 142 +

 143 +

 144 + async def _call_l9 ( job: ResearchJob ):

 145 + """Drive _layer9_claude_extraction until it reaches the existing filter.

 146 + We intercept the downstream call so we never touch the Claude API.

 147 + """

 148 + import tools.research_engine as re_mod

 149 +

 150 + async def fake_filter ( self ):

 151 + # Clear raw_pages to prevent the rest of L9 from running.

 152 + # We only care about observing raw_pages state AFTER the dedup block

 153 + # at the top of _layer9_claude_extraction.

 154 + self._observed_raw_pages = list (self.raw_pages)

 155 + self.raw_pages = []

 156 +

 157 + original = re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain

 158 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = fake_filter

 159 + try :

 160 + await job._layer9_claude_extraction()

 161 + finally :

 162 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = original

 163 +

 164 +

 165 + def _run_async ( coro ):

 166 + import asyncio

 167 + return asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(coro) if False else asyncio.run(coro)

 168 +

 169 +

 170 + def test_url_dedup_exact_duplicate ():

 171 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 172 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 173 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 174 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 175 + return "url_dedup_exact_duplicate"

 176 +

 177 +

 178 + def test_url_dedup_trailing_slash ():

 179 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 180 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 181 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 182 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 183 + return "url_dedup_trailing_slash"

 184 +

 185 +

 187 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 189 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 190 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 191 + return "url_dedup_case_insensitive"

 192 +

 193 +

 199 + ( "http://y.com/a" , "c" ),

 200 + ]

 220 + def _make_verified_contacts ( n: int ) -> list [ dict ]:

 221 + return [

 222 + _contact(

 223 + first= f"V {i} " ,

 224 + last= "Person" ,

 225 + email= f"v {i} @test.edu" ,

 226 + email_confidence= "VERIFIED" ,

 227 + )

 228 + for i in range (n)

 229 + ]

 230 +

 231 +

 232 + def test_l15_step5_skips_at_threshold ():

 233 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 234 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 15 )

 235 + # Reproduce only the skip predicate Part 1 Flag B adds, not the whole L15.

 236 + threshold = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 237 + verified = sum (

 238 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 239 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 240 + )

 241 + should_skip = threshold is not None and verified >= threshold

 242 + assert should_skip is True

 243 + return "l15_step5_skips_at_threshold"

 244 +

 245 +

 246 + def test_l15_step5_skips_above_threshold ():

 247 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 248 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 20 )

 249 + verified = sum (

 250 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 251 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 252 + )

 253 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 254 + assert should_skip is True

 255 + return "l15_step5_skips_above_threshold"

 256 +

 257 +

 258 + def test_l15_step5_runs_below_threshold ():

 259 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 260 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 14 )

 261 + verified = sum (

 262 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 263 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 264 + )

 265 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 266 + assert should_skip is False

 267 + return "l15_step5_runs_below_threshold"

 268 +

 269 +

 270 + def test_l15_step5_off_by_default ():

 271 + job = _fresh_job() # threshold defaults to None

 272 + assert job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is None

 273 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 100 )

 274 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None

 275 + assert should_skip is False

 276 + return "l15_step5_off_by_default"

 284 + class _FakeUsage :

 286 + self.input_tokens = input_tokens

 287 + self.output_tokens = output_tokens

 288 +

 289 +

 290 + class _FakeResponse :

 291 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 292 + self.usage = _FakeUsage(input_tokens, output_tokens)

 295 + def test_claude_usage_capture_when_enabled ():

 296 + start_usage_capture()

 297 + try :

 298 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 300 + finally :

 306 + assert records[ 0 ][ "model" ] == "claude-sonnet-4-6"

 332 + def test_claude_usage_exception_cleanup ():

 335 + try :

 337 + except RuntimeError:

 344 + return "claude_usage_exception_cleanup"

 676 + # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup. Lowercase + strip trailing slash.

 677 + # Catches the common L1/L2/L11/L16/L17 case where the same staff

 678 + # directory URL gets appended multiple times and would otherwise be

 679 + # sent to Claude 2-3x. Off by default — current v1 behavior unchanged.

 680 + if self.enable_url_dedup:

 681 + seen_urls: set [ str ] = set ()

 682 + deduped: list [ tuple [ str , str ]] = []

 683 + for url, content in self.raw_pages:

 684 + normalized = (url or "" ).rstrip( "/" ).lower()

 685 + if normalized and normalized in seen_urls:

 686 + continue

 687 + seen_urls.add(normalized)

 688 + deduped.append((url, content))

 689 + before = len (self.raw_pages)

 690 + self.raw_pages = deduped

 691 + if before > len (deduped):

 692 + logger.info(

 693 + f"L9 URL dedup: {before - len (deduped)} duplicate URLs "

 694 + f"removed ( {before} -> { len (deduped)} )"

 695 + )

 19 # Module-level cache: district_key → formatted known-contacts string

 20 _known_contacts_cache: dict [ str , str ] = {}

 22 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 23 + # Round 1 Flag C: Claude API usage capture

 24 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 25 + # Captures real input/output token counts from response.usage for A/B cost

 26 + # analysis. Module-level state is safe because:

 27 + # (a) CPython GIL makes list.append atomic, so cross-thread writes from

 28 + # run_in_executor workers don't corrupt the buffer

 29 + # (b) ResearchQueue processes jobs strictly one at a time, so there's no

 30 + # cross-job contamination of the flag or buffer

 31 + # Caller owns start/stop balance via try/finally so the flag never leaks.

 33 + _capture_usage_enabled: bool = False

 34 + _captured_usage: list [ dict ] = []

 35 +

 36 +

 37 + def start_usage_capture () -> None :

 38 + """Begin capturing Claude API usage. Pair with stop_usage_capture in a finally block."""

 39 + global _capture_usage_enabled, _captured_usage

 40 + _capture_usage_enabled = True

 41 + _captured_usage = []

 42 +

 43 +

 44 + def stop_usage_capture () -> list [ dict ]:

 45 + """Stop capture and return accumulated records. Safe to call multiple times."""

 46 + global _capture_usage_enabled

 47 + _capture_usage_enabled = False

 48 + records = list (_captured_usage)

 49 + _captured_usage.clear()

 50 + return records

 51 +

 52 +

 53 + def _log_usage ( response, source_url: str , model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6" ) -> None :

 54 + """Append one Claude call's token usage to the capture buffer if enabled. No-op otherwise."""

 55 + if not _capture_usage_enabled:

 56 + return

 57 + try :

 58 + _captured_usage.append({

 59 + "source_url" : source_url,

 60 + "input_tokens" : response.usage.input_tokens,

 61 + "output_tokens" : response.usage.output_tokens,

 62 + "model" : model,

 63 + })

 64 + except AttributeError:

 65 + pass

 66 +

 67 +

 68 def _get_known_contacts_for_district ( district_name: str ) -> str :

 69 """Load existing contacts for district from Leads tab. Cached per module lifetime."""

 70 if not district_name:

 135 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 136 + # Part 1 Flag A: URL dedup in L9

 137 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 138 +

 139 +

 140 + def _fresh_job ( **kwargs ) -> ResearchJob:

 141 + return ResearchJob( "Test ISD" , "TX" , **kwargs)

 142 +

 143 +

 144 + async def _call_l9 ( job: ResearchJob ):

 145 + """Drive _layer9_claude_extraction until it reaches the existing filter.

 146 + We intercept the downstream call so we never touch the Claude API.

 147 + """

 148 + import tools.research_engine as re_mod

 149 +

 150 + async def fake_filter ( self ):

 151 + # Clear raw_pages to prevent the rest of L9 from running.

 152 + # We only care about observing raw_pages state AFTER the dedup block

 153 + # at the top of _layer9_claude_extraction.

 154 + self._observed_raw_pages = list (self.raw_pages)

 155 + self.raw_pages = []

 156 +

 157 + original = re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain

 158 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = fake_filter

 159 + try :

 160 + await job._layer9_claude_extraction()

 161 + finally :

 162 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = original

 163 +

 164 +

 165 + def _run_async ( coro ):

 166 + import asyncio

 167 + return asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(coro) if False else asyncio.run(coro)

 168 +

 169 +

 170 + def test_url_dedup_exact_duplicate ():

 171 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 172 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 173 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 174 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 175 + return "url_dedup_exact_duplicate"

 176 +

 177 +

 178 + def test_url_dedup_trailing_slash ():

 179 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 180 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 181 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 182 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 183 + return "url_dedup_trailing_slash"

 184 +

 185 +

 186 + def test_url_dedup_case_insensitive ():

 187 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 188 + job.raw_pages = [( "HTTP://X.COM/STAFF" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 189 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 190 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 191 + return "url_dedup_case_insensitive"

 192 +

 193 +

 197 + ( "http://x.com/a" , "a" ),

 199 + ( "http://y.com/a" , "c" ),

 200 + ]

 201 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 204 +

 220 + def _make_verified_contacts ( n: int ) -> list [ dict ]:

 221 + return [

 222 + _contact(

 223 + first= f"V {i} " ,

 224 + last= "Person" ,

 225 + email= f"v {i} @test.edu" ,

 226 + email_confidence= "VERIFIED" ,

 227 + )

 228 + for i in range (n)

 229 + ]

 230 +

 231 +

 232 + def test_l15_step5_skips_at_threshold ():

 233 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 234 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 15 )

 235 + # Reproduce only the skip predicate Part 1 Flag B adds, not the whole L15.

 236 + threshold = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 237 + verified = sum (

 238 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 239 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 240 + )

 241 + should_skip = threshold is not None and verified >= threshold

 242 + assert should_skip is True

 243 + return "l15_step5_skips_at_threshold"

 244 +

 245 +

 246 + def test_l15_step5_skips_above_threshold ():

 247 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 248 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 20 )

 249 + verified = sum (

 250 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 251 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 252 + )

 253 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 254 + assert should_skip is True

 255 + return "l15_step5_skips_above_threshold"

 256 +

 257 +

 258 + def test_l15_step5_runs_below_threshold ():

 259 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 260 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 14 )

 261 + verified = sum (

 262 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 263 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 264 + )

 265 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 266 + assert should_skip is False

 267 + return "l15_step5_runs_below_threshold"

 268 +

 269 +

 270 + def test_l15_step5_off_by_default ():

 271 + job = _fresh_job() # threshold defaults to None

 272 + assert job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is None

 273 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 100 )

 274 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None

 275 + assert should_skip is False

 276 + return "l15_step5_off_by_default"

 289 +

 290 + class _FakeResponse :

 291 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 292 + self.usage = _FakeUsage(input_tokens, output_tokens)

 293 +

 294 +

 295 + def test_claude_usage_capture_when_enabled ():

 296 + start_usage_capture()

 297 + try :

 298 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 299 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 2000 , 800 ), "http://test/leadership" )

 300 + finally :

 301 + records = stop_usage_capture()

 304 + assert records[ 0 ][ "output_tokens" ] == 500

 306 + assert records[ 0 ][ "model" ] == "claude-sonnet-4-6"

 307 + assert records[ 1 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 2000

 308 + return "claude_usage_capture_when_enabled"

 309 +

 311 + def test_claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled ():

 319 +

 321 + def test_claude_usage_balanced_start_stop ():

 328 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 330 +

 332 + def test_claude_usage_exception_cleanup ():

 333 + start_usage_capture()

 334 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is True

 335 + try :

 336 + raise RuntimeError( "simulated mid-job exception" )

 337 + except RuntimeError:

 338 + stop_usage_capture() # mimics run() except-branch cleanup

 339 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 340 + # A fresh job must start with an empty buffer.

 341 + start_usage_capture()

 342 + assert ce._captured_usage == []

 343 + stop_usage_capture()

 344 + return "claude_usage_exception_cleanup"

 676 + # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup. Lowercase + strip trailing slash.

 677 + # Catches the common L1/L2/L11/L16/L17 case where the same staff

 678 + # directory URL gets appended multiple times and would otherwise be

 679 + # sent to Claude 2-3x. Off by default — current v1 behavior unchanged.

 680 + if self.enable_url_dedup:

 681 + seen_urls: set [ str ] = set ()

 682 + deduped: list [ tuple [ str , str ]] = []

 683 + for url, content in self.raw_pages:

 684 + normalized = (url or "" ).rstrip( "/" ).lower()

 685 + if normalized and normalized in seen_urls:

 686 + continue

 687 + seen_urls.add(normalized)

 688 + deduped.append((url, content))

 695 + )

 19 # Module-level cache: district_key → formatted known-contacts string

 20 _known_contacts_cache: dict [ str , str ] = {}

 22 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 23 + # Round 1 Flag C: Claude API usage capture

 24 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 25 + # Captures real input/output token counts from response.usage for A/B cost

 26 + # analysis. Module-level state is safe because:

 27 + # (a) CPython GIL makes list.append atomic, so cross-thread writes from

 28 + # run_in_executor workers don't corrupt the buffer

 29 + # (b) ResearchQueue processes jobs strictly one at a time, so there's no

 30 + # cross-job contamination of the flag or buffer

 31 + # Caller owns start/stop balance via try/finally so the flag never leaks.

 33 + _capture_usage_enabled: bool = False

 34 + _captured_usage: list [ dict ] = []

 35 +

 36 +

 37 + def start_usage_capture () -> None :

 38 + """Begin capturing Claude API usage. Pair with stop_usage_capture in a finally block."""

 39 + global _capture_usage_enabled, _captured_usage

 40 + _capture_usage_enabled = True

 41 + _captured_usage = []

 42 +

 43 +

 44 + def stop_usage_capture () -> list [ dict ]:

 45 + """Stop capture and return accumulated records. Safe to call multiple times."""

 46 + global _capture_usage_enabled

 47 + _capture_usage_enabled = False

 48 + records = list (_captured_usage)

 49 + _captured_usage.clear()

 50 + return records

 51 +

 52 +

 53 + def _log_usage ( response, source_url: str , model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6" ) -> None :

 54 + """Append one Claude call's token usage to the capture buffer if enabled. No-op otherwise."""

 55 + if not _capture_usage_enabled:

 56 + return

 57 + try :

 58 + _captured_usage.append({

 59 + "source_url" : source_url,

 60 + "input_tokens" : response.usage.input_tokens,

 61 + "output_tokens" : response.usage.output_tokens,

 62 + "model" : model,

 63 + })

 64 + except AttributeError:

 65 + pass

 66 +

 67 +

 68 def _get_known_contacts_for_district ( district_name: str ) -> str :

 69 """Load existing contacts for district from Leads tab. Cached per module lifetime."""

 70 if not district_name:

 135 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 136 + # Part 1 Flag A: URL dedup in L9

 137 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 138 +

 139 +

 140 + def _fresh_job ( **kwargs ) -> ResearchJob:

 141 + return ResearchJob( "Test ISD" , "TX" , **kwargs)

 142 +

 143 +

 144 + async def _call_l9 ( job: ResearchJob ):

 145 + """Drive _layer9_claude_extraction until it reaches the existing filter.

 146 + We intercept the downstream call so we never touch the Claude API.

 147 + """

 148 + import tools.research_engine as re_mod

 149 +

 150 + async def fake_filter ( self ):

 151 + # Clear raw_pages to prevent the rest of L9 from running.

 152 + # We only care about observing raw_pages state AFTER the dedup block

 153 + # at the top of _layer9_claude_extraction.

 154 + self._observed_raw_pages = list (self.raw_pages)

 155 + self.raw_pages = []

 156 +

 157 + original = re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain

 158 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = fake_filter

 159 + try :

 160 + await job._layer9_claude_extraction()

 161 + finally :

 162 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = original

 163 +

 164 +

 165 + def _run_async ( coro ):

 166 + import asyncio

 167 + return asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(coro) if False else asyncio.run(coro)

 168 +

 169 +

 170 + def test_url_dedup_exact_duplicate ():

 171 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 172 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 173 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 174 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 175 + return "url_dedup_exact_duplicate"

 176 +

 177 +

 178 + def test_url_dedup_trailing_slash ():

 179 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 180 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 181 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 182 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 183 + return "url_dedup_trailing_slash"

 184 +

 185 +

 186 + def test_url_dedup_case_insensitive ():

 187 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 188 + job.raw_pages = [( "HTTP://X.COM/STAFF" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 189 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 190 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 191 + return "url_dedup_case_insensitive"

 192 +

 193 +

 194 + def test_url_dedup_preserves_different_urls ():

 195 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 196 + job.raw_pages = [

 197 + ( "http://x.com/a" , "a" ),

 198 + ( "http://x.com/b" , "b" ),

 199 + ( "http://y.com/a" , "c" ),

 200 + ]

 201 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 202 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 3

 203 + return "url_dedup_preserves_different_urls"

 204 +

 205 +

 206 + def test_url_dedup_off_by_default ():

 207 + job = _fresh_job() # flag defaults to False

 208 + assert job.enable_url_dedup is False

 209 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com" , "b" )]

 210 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 211 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 2 , "dedup must NOT run when flag is off"

 212 + return "url_dedup_off_by_default"

 213 +

 215 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 216 + # Part 1 Flag B: L15 Step 5 skip threshold

 217 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 218 +

 219 +

 220 + def _make_verified_contacts ( n: int ) -> list [ dict ]:

 221 + return [

 222 + _contact(

 223 + first= f"V {i} " ,

 224 + last= "Person" ,

 225 + email= f"v {i} @test.edu" ,

 226 + email_confidence= "VERIFIED" ,

 227 + )

 228 + for i in range (n)

 229 + ]

 230 +

 231 +

 232 + def test_l15_step5_skips_at_threshold ():

 233 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 234 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 15 )

 235 + # Reproduce only the skip predicate Part 1 Flag B adds, not the whole L15.

 236 + threshold = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 237 + verified = sum (

 238 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 239 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 240 + )

 241 + should_skip = threshold is not None and verified >= threshold

 242 + assert should_skip is True

 243 + return "l15_step5_skips_at_threshold"

 244 +

 245 +

 246 + def test_l15_step5_skips_above_threshold ():

 247 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 248 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 20 )

 249 + verified = sum (

 250 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 251 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 252 + )

 253 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 254 + assert should_skip is True

 255 + return "l15_step5_skips_above_threshold"

 256 +

 257 +

 258 + def test_l15_step5_runs_below_threshold ():

 259 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 260 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 14 )

 261 + verified = sum (

 262 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 263 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 264 + )

 265 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 266 + assert should_skip is False

 267 + return "l15_step5_runs_below_threshold"

 268 +

 269 +

 270 + def test_l15_step5_off_by_default ():

 271 + job = _fresh_job() # threshold defaults to None

 272 + assert job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is None

 273 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 100 )

 274 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None

 275 + assert should_skip is False

 276 + return "l15_step5_off_by_default"

 277 +

 280 + # Part 1 Flag C: Claude usage capture

 284 + class _FakeUsage :

 285 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 286 + self.input_tokens = input_tokens

 287 + self.output_tokens = output_tokens

 288 +

 289 +

 290 + class _FakeResponse :

 291 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 292 + self.usage = _FakeUsage(input_tokens, output_tokens)

 293 +

 294 +

 295 + def test_claude_usage_capture_when_enabled ():

 296 + start_usage_capture()

 297 + try :

 298 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 299 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 2000 , 800 ), "http://test/leadership" )

 300 + finally :

 301 + records = stop_usage_capture()

 302 + assert len (records) == 2 , records

 303 + assert records[ 0 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 1000

 304 + assert records[ 0 ][ "output_tokens" ] == 500

 305 + assert records[ 0 ][ "source_url" ] == "http://test/staff"

 306 + assert records[ 0 ][ "model" ] == "claude-sonnet-4-6"

 307 + assert records[ 1 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 2000

 308 + return "claude_usage_capture_when_enabled"

 309 +

 310 +

 311 + def test_claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled ():

 312 + # Ensure flag is off (explicit reset in case a previous test leaked)

 337 + except RuntimeError:

 338 + stop_usage_capture() # mimics run() except-branch cleanup

 339 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 340 + # A fresh job must start with an empty buffer.

 341 + start_usage_capture()

 342 + assert ce._captured_usage == []

 343 + stop_usage_capture()

 344 + return "claude_usage_exception_cleanup"

 676 + # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup. Lowercase + strip trailing slash.

 678 + # directory URL gets appended multiple times and would otherwise be

 679 + # sent to Claude 2-3x. Off by default — current v1 behavior unchanged.

 681 + seen_urls: set [ str ] = set ()

 19 # Module-level cache: district_key → formatted known-contacts string

 20 _known_contacts_cache: dict [ str , str ] = {}

 22 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 23 + # Round 1 Flag C: Claude API usage capture

 24 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 25 + # Captures real input/output token counts from response.usage for A/B cost

 26 + # analysis. Module-level state is safe because:

 27 + # (a) CPython GIL makes list.append atomic, so cross-thread writes from

 28 + # run_in_executor workers don't corrupt the buffer

 29 + # (b) ResearchQueue processes jobs strictly one at a time, so there's no

 30 + # cross-job contamination of the flag or buffer

 31 + # Caller owns start/stop balance via try/finally so the flag never leaks.

 33 + _capture_usage_enabled: bool = False

 34 + _captured_usage: list [ dict ] = []

 35 +

 36 +

 37 + def start_usage_capture () -> None :

 38 + """Begin capturing Claude API usage. Pair with stop_usage_capture in a finally block."""

 39 + global _capture_usage_enabled, _captured_usage

 40 + _capture_usage_enabled = True

 41 + _captured_usage = []

 42 +

 43 +

 44 + def stop_usage_capture () -> list [ dict ]:

 45 + """Stop capture and return accumulated records. Safe to call multiple times."""

 46 + global _capture_usage_enabled

 47 + _capture_usage_enabled = False

 48 + records = list (_captured_usage)

 49 + _captured_usage.clear()

 50 + return records

 51 +

 52 +

 53 + def _log_usage ( response, source_url: str , model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6" ) -> None :

 54 + """Append one Claude call's token usage to the capture buffer if enabled. No-op otherwise."""

 55 + if not _capture_usage_enabled:

 56 + return

 57 + try :

 58 + _captured_usage.append({

 59 + "source_url" : source_url,

 60 + "input_tokens" : response.usage.input_tokens,

 61 + "output_tokens" : response.usage.output_tokens,

 62 + "model" : model,

 63 + })

 64 + except AttributeError:

 65 + pass

 66 +

 67 +

 68 def _get_known_contacts_for_district ( district_name: str ) -> str :

 69 """Load existing contacts for district from Leads tab. Cached per module lifetime."""

 70 if not district_name:

 135 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 136 + # Part 1 Flag A: URL dedup in L9

 139 +

 140 + def _fresh_job ( **kwargs ) -> ResearchJob:

 141 + return ResearchJob( "Test ISD" , "TX" , **kwargs)

 142 +

 143 +

 144 + async def _call_l9 ( job: ResearchJob ):

 145 + """Drive _layer9_claude_extraction until it reaches the existing filter.

 146 + We intercept the downstream call so we never touch the Claude API.

 147 + """

 148 + import tools.research_engine as re_mod

 149 +

 150 + async def fake_filter ( self ):

 151 + # Clear raw_pages to prevent the rest of L9 from running.

 152 + # We only care about observing raw_pages state AFTER the dedup block

 153 + # at the top of _layer9_claude_extraction.

 154 + self._observed_raw_pages = list (self.raw_pages)

 155 + self.raw_pages = []

 156 +

 157 + original = re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain

 158 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = fake_filter

 159 + try :

 160 + await job._layer9_claude_extraction()

 161 + finally :

 162 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = original

 163 +

 164 +

 165 + def _run_async ( coro ):

 166 + import asyncio

 167 + return asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(coro) if False else asyncio.run(coro)

 168 +

 169 +

 170 + def test_url_dedup_exact_duplicate ():

 171 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 172 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 173 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 174 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 175 + return "url_dedup_exact_duplicate"

 176 +

 177 +

 178 + def test_url_dedup_trailing_slash ():

 179 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 180 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 181 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 182 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 183 + return "url_dedup_trailing_slash"

 184 +

 185 +

 186 + def test_url_dedup_case_insensitive ():

 187 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 188 + job.raw_pages = [( "HTTP://X.COM/STAFF" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 189 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 190 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 191 + return "url_dedup_case_insensitive"

 192 +

 193 +

 194 + def test_url_dedup_preserves_different_urls ():

 195 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 196 + job.raw_pages = [

 197 + ( "http://x.com/a" , "a" ),

 198 + ( "http://x.com/b" , "b" ),

 199 + ( "http://y.com/a" , "c" ),

 200 + ]

 203 + return "url_dedup_preserves_different_urls"

 204 +

 206 + def test_url_dedup_off_by_default ():

 207 + job = _fresh_job() # flag defaults to False

 208 + assert job.enable_url_dedup is False

 209 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com" , "b" )]

 210 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 215 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 216 + # Part 1 Flag B: L15 Step 5 skip threshold

 217 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 218 +

 219 +

 220 + def _make_verified_contacts ( n: int ) -> list [ dict ]:

 221 + return [

 222 + _contact(

 223 + first= f"V {i} " ,

 224 + last= "Person" ,

 225 + email= f"v {i} @test.edu" ,

 226 + email_confidence= "VERIFIED" ,

 227 + )

 228 + for i in range (n)

 229 + ]

 230 +

 231 +

 232 + def test_l15_step5_skips_at_threshold ():

 233 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 234 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 15 )

 235 + # Reproduce only the skip predicate Part 1 Flag B adds, not the whole L15.

 236 + threshold = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 237 + verified = sum (

 238 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 239 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 240 + )

 241 + should_skip = threshold is not None and verified >= threshold

 242 + assert should_skip is True

 243 + return "l15_step5_skips_at_threshold"

 244 +

 245 +

 246 + def test_l15_step5_skips_above_threshold ():

 247 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 248 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 20 )

 249 + verified = sum (

 250 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 251 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 252 + )

 253 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 254 + assert should_skip is True

 255 + return "l15_step5_skips_above_threshold"

 256 +

 257 +

 258 + def test_l15_step5_runs_below_threshold ():

 259 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 260 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 14 )

 261 + verified = sum (

 262 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 263 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 264 + )

 265 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 266 + assert should_skip is False

 267 + return "l15_step5_runs_below_threshold"

 268 +

 269 +

 270 + def test_l15_step5_off_by_default ():

 271 + job = _fresh_job() # threshold defaults to None

 272 + assert job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is None

 273 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 100 )

 274 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None

 275 + assert should_skip is False

 276 + return "l15_step5_off_by_default"

 277 +

 281 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 282 +

 283 +

 284 + class _FakeUsage :

 285 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 286 + self.input_tokens = input_tokens

 287 + self.output_tokens = output_tokens

 288 +

 289 +

 290 + class _FakeResponse :

 291 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 292 + self.usage = _FakeUsage(input_tokens, output_tokens)

 293 +

 294 +

 295 + def test_claude_usage_capture_when_enabled ():

 296 + start_usage_capture()

 297 + try :

 298 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 299 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 2000 , 800 ), "http://test/leadership" )

 300 + finally :

 301 + records = stop_usage_capture()

 302 + assert len (records) == 2 , records

 303 + assert records[ 0 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 1000

 304 + assert records[ 0 ][ "output_tokens" ] == 500

 305 + assert records[ 0 ][ "source_url" ] == "http://test/staff"

 306 + assert records[ 0 ][ "model" ] == "claude-sonnet-4-6"

 307 + assert records[ 1 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 2000

 308 + return "claude_usage_capture_when_enabled"

 309 +

 310 +

 311 + def test_claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled ():

 312 + # Ensure flag is off (explicit reset in case a previous test leaked)

 313 + stop_usage_capture()

 314 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 315 + # No start_usage_capture was called — buffer should be empty

 316 + assert ce._captured_usage == [], ce._captured_usage

 317 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 318 + return "claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled"

 319 +

 320 +

 321 + def test_claude_usage_balanced_start_stop ():

 322 + start_usage_capture()

 323 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test" )

 324 + first = stop_usage_capture()

 325 + second = stop_usage_capture()

 326 + assert len (first) == 1

 327 + assert second == [], "second stop must return empty after buffer is cleared"

 328 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 329 + return "claude_usage_balanced_start_stop"

 330 +

 331 +

 332 + def test_claude_usage_exception_cleanup ():

 333 + start_usage_capture()

 334 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is True

 335 + try :

 336 + raise RuntimeError( "simulated mid-job exception" )

 337 + except RuntimeError:

 338 + stop_usage_capture() # mimics run() except-branch cleanup

 339 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 340 + # A fresh job must start with an empty buffer.

 341 + start_usage_capture()

 342 + assert ce._captured_usage == []

 343 + stop_usage_capture()

 344 + return "claude_usage_exception_cleanup"

 345 +

 19 # Module-level cache: district_key → formatted known-contacts string

 22 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 23 + # Round 1 Flag C: Claude API usage capture

 24 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 25 + # Captures real input/output token counts from response.usage for A/B cost

 26 + # analysis. Module-level state is safe because:

 27 + # (a) CPython GIL makes list.append atomic, so cross-thread writes from

 28 + # run_in_executor workers don't corrupt the buffer

 29 + # (b) ResearchQueue processes jobs strictly one at a time, so there's no

 30 + # cross-job contamination of the flag or buffer

 31 + # Caller owns start/stop balance via try/finally so the flag never leaks.

 33 + _capture_usage_enabled: bool = False

 34 + _captured_usage: list [ dict ] = []

 35 +

 36 +

 37 + def start_usage_capture () -> None :

 38 + """Begin capturing Claude API usage. Pair with stop_usage_capture in a finally block."""

 39 + global _capture_usage_enabled, _captured_usage

 40 + _capture_usage_enabled = True

 41 + _captured_usage = []

 42 +

 43 +

 44 + def stop_usage_capture () -> list [ dict ]:

 45 + """Stop capture and return accumulated records. Safe to call multiple times."""

 46 + global _capture_usage_enabled

 47 + _capture_usage_enabled = False

 48 + records = list (_captured_usage)

 49 + _captured_usage.clear()

 50 + return records

 51 +

 52 +

 53 + def _log_usage ( response, source_url: str , model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6" ) -> None :

 54 + """Append one Claude call's token usage to the capture buffer if enabled. No-op otherwise."""

 55 + if not _capture_usage_enabled:

 56 + return

 57 + try :

 58 + _captured_usage.append({

 59 + "source_url" : source_url,

 60 + "input_tokens" : response.usage.input_tokens,

 61 + "output_tokens" : response.usage.output_tokens,

 62 + "model" : model,

 63 + })

 64 + except AttributeError:

 65 + pass

 66 +

 67 +

 69 """Load existing contacts for district from Leads tab. Cached per module lifetime."""

 143 +

 144 + async def _call_l9 ( job: ResearchJob ):

 145 + """Drive _layer9_claude_extraction until it reaches the existing filter.

 146 + We intercept the downstream call so we never touch the Claude API.

 147 + """

 148 + import tools.research_engine as re_mod

 149 +

 150 + async def fake_filter ( self ):

 151 + # Clear raw_pages to prevent the rest of L9 from running.

 152 + # We only care about observing raw_pages state AFTER the dedup block

 153 + # at the top of _layer9_claude_extraction.

 154 + self._observed_raw_pages = list (self.raw_pages)

 155 + self.raw_pages = []

 156 +

 157 + original = re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain

 158 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = fake_filter

 159 + try :

 160 + await job._layer9_claude_extraction()

 161 + finally :

 162 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = original

 163 +

 164 +

 165 + def _run_async ( coro ):

 166 + import asyncio

 167 + return asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(coro) if False else asyncio.run(coro)

 168 +

 169 +

 170 + def test_url_dedup_exact_duplicate ():

 171 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 172 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 173 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 174 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 175 + return "url_dedup_exact_duplicate"

 176 +

 177 +

 178 + def test_url_dedup_trailing_slash ():

 179 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 180 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 181 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 182 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 183 + return "url_dedup_trailing_slash"

 184 +

 185 +

 186 + def test_url_dedup_case_insensitive ():

 187 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 188 + job.raw_pages = [( "HTTP://X.COM/STAFF" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 189 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 190 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 191 + return "url_dedup_case_insensitive"

 192 +

 193 +

 194 + def test_url_dedup_preserves_different_urls ():

 195 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 196 + job.raw_pages = [

 197 + ( "http://x.com/a" , "a" ),

 198 + ( "http://x.com/b" , "b" ),

 199 + ( "http://y.com/a" , "c" ),

 200 + ]

 201 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 202 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 3

 203 + return "url_dedup_preserves_different_urls"

 204 +

 205 +

 206 + def test_url_dedup_off_by_default ():

 207 + job = _fresh_job() # flag defaults to False

 208 + assert job.enable_url_dedup is False

 209 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com" , "b" )]

 210 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 211 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 2 , "dedup must NOT run when flag is off"

 212 + return "url_dedup_off_by_default"

 213 +

 214 +

 215 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 216 + # Part 1 Flag B: L15 Step 5 skip threshold

 217 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 218 +

 219 +

 220 + def _make_verified_contacts ( n: int ) -> list [ dict ]:

 221 + return [

 222 + _contact(

 223 + first= f"V {i} " ,

 224 + last= "Person" ,

 225 + email= f"v {i} @test.edu" ,

 226 + email_confidence= "VERIFIED" ,

 227 + )

 228 + for i in range (n)

 229 + ]

 230 +

 231 +

 232 + def test_l15_step5_skips_at_threshold ():

 233 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 234 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 15 )

 235 + # Reproduce only the skip predicate Part 1 Flag B adds, not the whole L15.

 236 + threshold = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 237 + verified = sum (

 238 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 239 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 240 + )

 241 + should_skip = threshold is not None and verified >= threshold

 242 + assert should_skip is True

 243 + return "l15_step5_skips_at_threshold"

 244 +

 245 +

 246 + def test_l15_step5_skips_above_threshold ():

 247 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 248 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 20 )

 249 + verified = sum (

 250 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 251 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 252 + )

 253 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 254 + assert should_skip is True

 255 + return "l15_step5_skips_above_threshold"

 256 +

 257 +

 258 + def test_l15_step5_runs_below_threshold ():

 259 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 260 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 14 )

 261 + verified = sum (

 262 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 263 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 264 + )

 265 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 266 + assert should_skip is False

 267 + return "l15_step5_runs_below_threshold"

 268 +

 269 +

 270 + def test_l15_step5_off_by_default ():

 271 + job = _fresh_job() # threshold defaults to None

 272 + assert job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is None

 273 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 100 )

 274 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None

 275 + assert should_skip is False

 276 + return "l15_step5_off_by_default"

 277 +

 278 +

 279 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 280 + # Part 1 Flag C: Claude usage capture

 281 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 282 +

 283 +

 284 + class _FakeUsage :

 285 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 286 + self.input_tokens = input_tokens

 287 + self.output_tokens = output_tokens

 288 +

 289 +

 290 + class _FakeResponse :

 291 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 292 + self.usage = _FakeUsage(input_tokens, output_tokens)

 293 +

 294 +

 295 + def test_claude_usage_capture_when_enabled ():

 296 + start_usage_capture()

 297 + try :

 298 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 299 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 2000 , 800 ), "http://test/leadership" )

 300 + finally :

 301 + records = stop_usage_capture()

 302 + assert len (records) == 2 , records

 303 + assert records[ 0 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 1000

 304 + assert records[ 0 ][ "output_tokens" ] == 500

 305 + assert records[ 0 ][ "source_url" ] == "http://test/staff"

 306 + assert records[ 0 ][ "model" ] == "claude-sonnet-4-6"

 307 + assert records[ 1 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 2000

 308 + return "claude_usage_capture_when_enabled"

 309 +

 310 +

 311 + def test_claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled ():

 312 + # Ensure flag is off (explicit reset in case a previous test leaked)

 313 + stop_usage_capture()

 314 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 315 + # No start_usage_capture was called — buffer should be empty

 316 + assert ce._captured_usage == [], ce._captured_usage

 317 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 318 + return "claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled"

 319 +

 320 +

 321 + def test_claude_usage_balanced_start_stop ():

 322 + start_usage_capture()

 323 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test" )

 324 + first = stop_usage_capture()

 325 + second = stop_usage_capture()

 326 + assert len (first) == 1

 327 + assert second == [], "second stop must return empty after buffer is cleared"

 328 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 329 + return "claude_usage_balanced_start_stop"

 330 +

 331 +

 332 + def test_claude_usage_exception_cleanup ():

 333 + start_usage_capture()

 334 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is True

 335 + try :

 336 + raise RuntimeError( "simulated mid-job exception" )

 337 + except RuntimeError:

 338 + stop_usage_capture() # mimics run() except-branch cleanup

 339 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 340 + # A fresh job must start with an empty buffer.

 341 + start_usage_capture()

 342 + assert ce._captured_usage == []

 343 + stop_usage_capture()

 344 + return "claude_usage_exception_cleanup"

 345 +

 346 +

 22 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 23 + # Round 1 Flag C: Claude API usage capture

 24 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 25 + # Captures real input/output token counts from response.usage for A/B cost

 26 + # analysis. Module-level state is safe because:

 27 + # (a) CPython GIL makes list.append atomic, so cross-thread writes from

 28 + # run_in_executor workers don't corrupt the buffer

 29 + # (b) ResearchQueue processes jobs strictly one at a time, so there's no

 30 + # cross-job contamination of the flag or buffer

 31 + # Caller owns start/stop balance via try/finally so the flag never leaks.

 33 + _capture_usage_enabled: bool = False

 34 + _captured_usage: list [ dict ] = []

 35 +

 36 +

 37 + def start_usage_capture () -> None :

 38 + """Begin capturing Claude API usage. Pair with stop_usage_capture in a finally block."""

 39 + global _capture_usage_enabled, _captured_usage

 40 + _capture_usage_enabled = True

 41 + _captured_usage = []

 42 +

 43 +

 44 + def stop_usage_capture () -> list [ dict ]:

 45 + """Stop capture and return accumulated records. Safe to call multiple times."""

 46 + global _capture_usage_enabled

 47 + _capture_usage_enabled = False

 48 + records = list (_captured_usage)

 49 + _captured_usage.clear()

 50 + return records

 51 +

 52 +

 53 + def _log_usage ( response, source_url: str , model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6" ) -> None :

 54 + """Append one Claude call's token usage to the capture buffer if enabled. No-op otherwise."""

 55 + if not _capture_usage_enabled:

 56 + return

 57 + try :

 58 + _captured_usage.append({

 59 + "source_url" : source_url,

 60 + "input_tokens" : response.usage.input_tokens,

 61 + "output_tokens" : response.usage.output_tokens,

 62 + "model" : model,

 63 + })

 64 + except AttributeError:

 65 + pass

 66 +

 67 +

 69 """Load existing contacts for district from Leads tab. Cached per module lifetime."""

 172 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 173 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 174 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 175 + return "url_dedup_exact_duplicate"

 176 +

 177 +

 178 + def test_url_dedup_trailing_slash ():

 179 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 181 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 182 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 183 + return "url_dedup_trailing_slash"

 186 + def test_url_dedup_case_insensitive ():

 187 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 189 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 190 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 191 + return "url_dedup_case_insensitive"

 192 +

 193 +

 194 + def test_url_dedup_preserves_different_urls ():

 196 + job.raw_pages = [

 197 + ( "http://x.com/a" , "a" ),

 198 + ( "http://x.com/b" , "b" ),

 199 + ( "http://y.com/a" , "c" ),

 200 + ]

 201 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 202 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 3

 203 + return "url_dedup_preserves_different_urls"

 204 +

 205 +

 206 + def test_url_dedup_off_by_default ():

 207 + job = _fresh_job() # flag defaults to False

 208 + assert job.enable_url_dedup is False

 209 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com" , "b" )]

 210 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 211 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 2 , "dedup must NOT run when flag is off"

 215 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 216 + # Part 1 Flag B: L15 Step 5 skip threshold

 217 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 218 +

 219 +

 220 + def _make_verified_contacts ( n: int ) -> list [ dict ]:

 221 + return [

 222 + _contact(

 223 + first= f"V {i} " ,

 224 + last= "Person" ,

 225 + email= f"v {i} @test.edu" ,

 226 + email_confidence= "VERIFIED" ,

 227 + )

 228 + for i in range (n)

 229 + ]

 230 +

 231 +

 232 + def test_l15_step5_skips_at_threshold ():

 233 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 234 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 15 )

 235 + # Reproduce only the skip predicate Part 1 Flag B adds, not the whole L15.

 236 + threshold = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 237 + verified = sum (

 238 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 239 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 240 + )

 241 + should_skip = threshold is not None and verified >= threshold

 242 + assert should_skip is True

 243 + return "l15_step5_skips_at_threshold"

 244 +

 245 +

 246 + def test_l15_step5_skips_above_threshold ():

 247 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 248 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 20 )

 249 + verified = sum (

 250 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 251 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 252 + )

 253 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 254 + assert should_skip is True

 255 + return "l15_step5_skips_above_threshold"

 256 +

 257 +

 258 + def test_l15_step5_runs_below_threshold ():

 259 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 260 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 14 )

 261 + verified = sum (

 262 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 263 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 264 + )

 265 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 266 + assert should_skip is False

 267 + return "l15_step5_runs_below_threshold"

 268 +

 269 +

 270 + def test_l15_step5_off_by_default ():

 271 + job = _fresh_job() # threshold defaults to None

 272 + assert job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is None

 273 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 100 )

 274 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None

 275 + assert should_skip is False

 276 + return "l15_step5_off_by_default"

 277 +

 279 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 280 + # Part 1 Flag C: Claude usage capture

 281 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 282 +

 283 +

 284 + class _FakeUsage :

 285 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 286 + self.input_tokens = input_tokens

 287 + self.output_tokens = output_tokens

 288 +

 289 +

 290 + class _FakeResponse :

 291 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 292 + self.usage = _FakeUsage(input_tokens, output_tokens)

 293 +

 294 +

 295 + def test_claude_usage_capture_when_enabled ():

 296 + start_usage_capture()

 297 + try :

 298 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 299 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 2000 , 800 ), "http://test/leadership" )

 300 + finally :

 301 + records = stop_usage_capture()

 302 + assert len (records) == 2 , records

 303 + assert records[ 0 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 1000

 304 + assert records[ 0 ][ "output_tokens" ] == 500

 305 + assert records[ 0 ][ "source_url" ] == "http://test/staff"

 306 + assert records[ 0 ][ "model" ] == "claude-sonnet-4-6"

 307 + assert records[ 1 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 2000

 308 + return "claude_usage_capture_when_enabled"

 309 +

 310 +

 311 + def test_claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled ():

 312 + # Ensure flag is off (explicit reset in case a previous test leaked)

 313 + stop_usage_capture()

 314 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 315 + # No start_usage_capture was called — buffer should be empty

 316 + assert ce._captured_usage == [], ce._captured_usage

 317 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 318 + return "claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled"

 319 +

 320 +

 321 + def test_claude_usage_balanced_start_stop ():

 322 + start_usage_capture()

 323 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test" )

 324 + first = stop_usage_capture()

 325 + second = stop_usage_capture()

 326 + assert len (first) == 1

 327 + assert second == [], "second stop must return empty after buffer is cleared"

 328 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 329 + return "claude_usage_balanced_start_stop"

 330 +

 331 +

 332 + def test_claude_usage_exception_cleanup ():

 333 + start_usage_capture()

 334 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is True

 335 + try :

 336 + raise RuntimeError( "simulated mid-job exception" )

 337 + except RuntimeError:

 338 + stop_usage_capture() # mimics run() except-branch cleanup

 339 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 340 + # A fresh job must start with an empty buffer.

 341 + start_usage_capture()

 342 + assert ce._captured_usage == []

 343 + stop_usage_capture()

 344 + return "claude_usage_exception_cleanup"

 345 +

 346 +

 253 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 254 + assert should_skip is True

 255 + return "l15_step5_skips_above_threshold"

 256 +

 257 +

 258 + def test_l15_step5_runs_below_threshold ():

 260 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 14 )

 261 + verified = sum (

 262 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 263 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 264 + )

 266 + assert should_skip is False

 269 +

 271 + job = _fresh_job() # threshold defaults to None

 272 + assert job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is None

 275 + assert should_skip is False

 279 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 280 + # Part 1 Flag C: Claude usage capture

 281 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 282 +

 283 +

 284 + class _FakeUsage :

 285 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 286 + self.input_tokens = input_tokens

 287 + self.output_tokens = output_tokens

 288 +

 289 +

 290 + class _FakeResponse :

 291 + def __init__ ( self, input_tokens: int , output_tokens: int ):

 292 + self.usage = _FakeUsage(input_tokens, output_tokens)

 293 +

 294 +

 295 + def test_claude_usage_capture_when_enabled ():

 296 + start_usage_capture()

 297 + try :

 298 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 299 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 2000 , 800 ), "http://test/leadership" )

 300 + finally :

 301 + records = stop_usage_capture()

 302 + assert len (records) == 2 , records

 303 + assert records[ 0 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 1000

 304 + assert records[ 0 ][ "output_tokens" ] == 500

 305 + assert records[ 0 ][ "source_url" ] == "http://test/staff"

 306 + assert records[ 0 ][ "model" ] == "claude-sonnet-4-6"

 307 + assert records[ 1 ][ "input_tokens" ] == 2000

 308 + return "claude_usage_capture_when_enabled"

 309 +

 310 +

 311 + def test_claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled ():

 312 + # Ensure flag is off (explicit reset in case a previous test leaked)

 313 + stop_usage_capture()

 314 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test/staff" )

 315 + # No start_usage_capture was called — buffer should be empty

 316 + assert ce._captured_usage == [], ce._captured_usage

 317 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 318 + return "claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled"

 319 +

 320 +

 321 + def test_claude_usage_balanced_start_stop ():

 322 + start_usage_capture()

 323 + _log_usage(_FakeResponse( 1000 , 500 ), "http://test" )

 324 + first = stop_usage_capture()

 325 + second = stop_usage_capture()

 326 + assert len (first) == 1

 327 + assert second == [], "second stop must return empty after buffer is cleared"

 328 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 329 + return "claude_usage_balanced_start_stop"

 330 +

 331 +

 332 + def test_claude_usage_exception_cleanup ():

 333 + start_usage_capture()

 334 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is True

 335 + try :

 336 + raise RuntimeError( "simulated mid-job exception" )

 337 + except RuntimeError:

 338 + stop_usage_capture() # mimics run() except-branch cleanup

 339 + assert ce._capture_usage_enabled is False

 340 + # A fresh job must start with an empty buffer.

 341 + start_usage_capture()

 342 + assert ce._captured_usage == []

 343 + stop_usage_capture()

 344 + return "claude_usage_exception_cleanup"

 345 +

 346 +

 ⏺ Root cause found: _add_raw_from_serper appends each Serper hit with different snippet text per query, and other layers (L6 fetch, L18 Firecrawl, L16/L17 Exa) append the same URL with full-page content . My
 URL-only dedup keeps the first occurrence, often throwing away richer content. Fix: dedup by URL but keep the longest content for each URL.

 135 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 136 + # Part 1 Flag A: URL dedup in L9

 137 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 138 +

 139 +

 140 + def _fresh_job ( **kwargs ) -> ResearchJob:

 141 + return ResearchJob( "Test ISD" , "TX" , **kwargs)

 142 +

 143 +

 144 + async def _call_l9 ( job: ResearchJob ):

 145 + """Drive _layer9_claude_extraction until it reaches the existing filter.

 146 + We intercept the downstream call so we never touch the Claude API.

 147 + """

 148 + import tools.research_engine as re_mod

 149 +

 150 + async def fake_filter ( self ):

 151 + # Clear raw_pages to prevent the rest of L9 from running.

 152 + # We only care about observing raw_pages state AFTER the dedup block

 153 + # at the top of _layer9_claude_extraction.

 154 + self._observed_raw_pages = list (self.raw_pages)

 155 + self.raw_pages = []

 156 +

 157 + original = re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain

 158 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = fake_filter

 159 + try :

 160 + await job._layer9_claude_extraction()

 161 + finally :

 162 + re_mod.ResearchJob._filter_raw_pages_by_domain = original

 163 +

 164 +

 165 + def _run_async ( coro ):

 166 + import asyncio

 167 + return asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(coro) if False else asyncio.run(coro)

 168 +

 169 +

 170 + def test_url_dedup_exact_duplicate ():

 171 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 172 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 173 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 174 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 175 + return "url_dedup_exact_duplicate"

 176 +

 177 +

 178 + def test_url_dedup_trailing_slash ():

 179 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 180 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/staff/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 181 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 182 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 183 + return "url_dedup_trailing_slash"

 184 +

 185 +

 186 + def test_url_dedup_case_insensitive ():

 187 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 188 + job.raw_pages = [( "HTTP://X.COM/STAFF" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com/staff" , "b" )]

 189 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 190 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 1

 191 + return "url_dedup_case_insensitive"

 192 +

 193 +

 194 + def test_url_dedup_preserves_different_urls ():

 195 + job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 196 + job.raw_pages = [

 197 + ( "http://x.com/a" , "a" ),

 198 + ( "http://x.com/b" , "b" ),

 199 + ( "http://y.com/a" , "c" ),

 200 + ]

 201 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 202 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 3

 203 + return "url_dedup_preserves_different_urls"

 204 +

 205 +

 206 + def test_url_dedup_off_by_default ():

 207 + job = _fresh_job() # flag defaults to False

 208 + assert job.enable_url_dedup is False

 209 + job.raw_pages = [( "http://x.com/" , "a" ), ( "http://x.com" , "b" )]

 210 + _run_async(_call_l9(job))

 211 + assert len (job._observed_raw_pages) == 2 , "dedup must NOT run when flag is off"

 212 + return "url_dedup_off_by_default"
