 219 +

 220 + def _make_verified_contacts ( n: int ) -> list [ dict ]:

 221 + return [

 222 + _contact(

 223 + first= f"V {i} " ,

 224 + last= "Person" ,

 225 + email= f"v {i} @test.edu" ,

 226 + email_confidence= "VERIFIED" ,

 227 + )

 228 + for i in range (n)

 229 + ]

 230 +

 231 +

 232 + def test_l15_step5_skips_at_threshold ():

 233 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 234 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 15 )

 235 + # Reproduce only the skip predicate Part 1 Flag B adds, not the whole L15.

 236 + threshold = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 237 + verified = sum (

 238 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 239 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 240 + )

 241 + should_skip = threshold is not None and verified >= threshold

 242 + assert should_skip is True

 243 + return "l15_step5_skips_at_threshold"

 244 +

 245 +

 247 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 249 + verified = sum (

 250 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 251 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 252 + )

 253 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 254 + assert should_skip is True

 255 + return "l15_step5_skips_above_threshold"

 256 +

 257 +

 259 + job = _fresh_job(l15_step5_skip_threshold= 15 )

 260 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 14 )

 261 + verified = sum (

 262 + 1 for c in job.all_contacts

 263 + if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 264 + )

 265 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None and verified >= job.l15_step5_skip_threshold

 266 + assert should_skip is False

 267 + return "l15_step5_runs_below_threshold"

 268 +

 269 +

 270 + def test_l15_step5_off_by_default ():

 271 + job = _fresh_job() # threshold defaults to None

 272 + assert job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is None

 273 + job.all_contacts = _make_verified_contacts( 100 )

 274 + should_skip = job.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None

 275 + assert should_skip is False

 276 + return "l15_step5_off_by_default"

 277 +

 ⏺ Gate failed — v2 lost 11 verified contacts (30→19). URL dedup is too aggressive. Let me check _add_raw_from_serper to understand the root cause.

 ⏺ Root cause found: _add_raw_from_serper appends each Serper hit with different snippet text per query, and other layers (L6 fetch, L18 Firecrawl, L16/L17 Exa) append the same URL with full-page content . My

 URL-only dedup keeps the first occurrence, often throwing away richer content. Fix: dedup by URL but keep the longest content for each URL.

 Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ Gate failed — v2 lost 11 verified contacts (30→19). URL dedup is too aggressive. Let me check _add_raw_from_serper to understand the root cause.

 ⏺ Root cause found: _add_raw_from_serper appends each Serper hit with different snippet text per query, and other layers (L6 fetch, L18 Firecrawl, L16/L17 Exa) append the same URL with full-page content . My

 URL-only dedup keeps the first occurrence, often throwing away richer content. Fix: dedup by URL but keep the longest content for each URL.

 ⏺ Update ( tools/research_engine.py )

 ⎿   Added 21 lines, removed 13 lines

 689 logger.warning( f"No raw pages to extract from for {self.district_name} " )

 690 return

 692 - # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup. Lowercase + strip trailing slash.

 693 - # Catches the common L1/L2/L11/L16/L17 case where the same staff

 694 - # directory URL gets appended multiple times and would otherwise be

 695 - # sent to Claude 2-3x. Off by default — current v1 behavior unchanged.

 692 + # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup with longest-content-wins.

 693 + # Serper snippets get appended per-query with different snippet text

 694 + # for the same URL, and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers append

 695 + # the same URL with full-page HTML. Naive first-occurrence dedup keeps

 696 + # the short snippet and throws away the full page — a quality loss.

 697 + # "Keep the longest content per normalized URL" preserves the richest

 698 + # page while still killing the duplicate-extraction cost.

 699 + # Normalization: lowercase + strip trailing slash. Off by default.

 700 if self.enable_url_dedup:

 697 - seen_urls: set[str] = set()

 698 - deduped : list[ tuple[ str , str ] ] = []

 701 + by_url: dict [ str , tuple [ str , str ]] = {}

 702 + order : list [ str ] = []

 703 for url, content in self.raw_pages:

 704 normalized = (url or "" ).rstrip( "/" ).lower()

 701 - if normalized and normalized in seen_urls :

 705 + if not normalized:

 706 continue

 703 - seen_urls . add (normalized)

 704 - deduped.append((url, content))

 707 + existing = by_url . get (normalized)

 708 + if existing is None :

 709 + by_url[normalized] = (url, content)

 710 + order.append(normalized)

 711 + elif len (content or "" ) > len (existing[ 1 ] or "" ):

 712 + by_url[normalized] = (url, content)

 713 before = len (self.raw_pages)

 706 - self.raw_pages = deduped

 707 - if before > len( deduped ):

 714 + self.raw_pages = [by_url[k] for k in order]

 715 + if before > len ( self.raw_pages ):

 716 logger.info(

 709 - f"L9 URL dedup: {before - len( deduped )} duplicate URLs "

 710 - f" removed ({before} -> {len( deduped )})"

 717 + f"L9 URL dedup: {before - len ( self.raw_pages )} duplicate URLs "

 718 + f" collapsed ( longest content wins; {before} -> { len ( self.raw_pages )} )"

 719 )

 168 def test_url_dedup_exact_duplicate ():

 169 + # When two pages share a normalized URL, keep the one with LONGER content

 170 + # (preserves full fetched page over snippet).

 171 job = _fresh_job(enable_url_dedup= True )

 170 - job.raw_pages = [("http://x.com/staff", "a"), ("http://x.com/staff", "b")]

 ⏺ Gate failed — v2 lost 11 verified contacts (30→19). URL dedup is too aggressive. Let me check _add_raw_from_serper to understand the root cause.

 ⏺ Root cause found: _add_raw_from_serper appends each Serper hit with different snippet text per query, and other layers (L6 fetch, L18 Firecrawl, L16/L17 Exa) append the same URL with full-page content . My

 URL-only dedup keeps the first occurrence, often throwing away richer content. Fix: dedup by URL but keep the longest content for each URL.

 692 + # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup with longest-content-wins.

 693 + # Serper snippets get appended per-query with different snippet text

 694 + # for the same URL, and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers append

 695 + # the same URL with full-page HTML. Naive first-occurrence dedup keeps

 696 + # the short snippet and throws away the full page — a quality loss.

 697 + # "Keep the longest content per normalized URL" preserves the richest

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 57 lines
 6 ## CURRENT STATE — update this after each session
 8 + **Session 61 shipped Research Engine Round 1 code across 4 commits. Level 3 Waverly A/B failed the verified_quality gate; Level 3 halted at target 1 per plan's "stop on first failure" rule. Flag
 + s default OFF so production is unchanged — v1 behavior is byte-for-byte preserved.**
 9 +

 10 + ### Session 61 commits (all on main)

 11 +

 12 + | commit | scope |

 13 + |---|---|

 14 + | `978499b` | Part 0: shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision helper in contact_extractor, rewire extract_from_multiple + ResearchJob._merge_contacts). Level 2 Waverly check: 37/37 contacts prese

 + rved, zero regression. 7 unit tests green. |

 15 + | `bd7a562` | Part 1: three feature flags on ResearchJob.__init__ (enable_url_dedup, l15_step5_skip_threshold, log_claude_usage). All default OFF. Module-level Claude usage capture in contact_ex

 + tractor.py is safe across run_in_executor thread boundaries because CPython GIL + serial ResearchQueue guarantee. 20 unit tests green. |

 16 + | `21d9b3b` | Part 2: A/B harness (scripts/ab_research_engine.py + ab_analyze.py). Measurement-grade cost from response.usage token counts. --max-cost-usd ceiling. 3 numerical gates. |

 17 + | `e62f4be` | Round 1.1 patch: URL dedup keeps longest content per URL (first-occurrence rule lost full-page content to short Serper snippets). 21 unit tests green. |

 18 +

 19 + ### Level 3 Waverly A/B — FAIL, stopped at target 1

 20 +

 21 + Two runs against "Waverly School District 145" NE:

 22 +

 23 + | metric | v1 baseline | v2 flags-on (first-wins) | v2 flags-on (longest-wins) |

 24 + |---|---|---|---|

 25 + | contacts total | 34 / 36 | 20 | 23 |

 26 + | contacts verified | **30 / 30** | **19** | **22** |

 27 + | claude calls | 134 / 137 | 65 | 69 |

 28 + | wall clock (s) | 334 / 329 | 171 | 179 |

 29 + | cost usd (measurement) | $0.80 / $0.81 | $0.41 | $0.44 |

 30 + | verified_quality_gate (≥95%) | — | **FAIL** (63%) | **FAIL** (73%) |

 31 + | cost_reduction_gate (≤90%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 32 + | wall_clock_gate (≤105%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 33 +

 34 + **Root cause of verified loss** (13 unique contacts in v1 that v2 missed):

 35 +

 36 + - **URL dedup is lossy for Serper snippets.** Each `_add_raw_from_serper` call appends per-query with different snippet text — the same URL gets 2-4 distinct snippet entries where each snippet h

 + ighlights sentences matching that query. Dedup by URL (either first-wins OR longest-wins) collapses those to one entry and loses the distinct per-query highlighting. Longest-wins recovered ~3 co

 + ntacts vs first-wins, but ~8 contacts remain lost.

 37 + - **L15 Step 5 skip at threshold=15** drops discovery search contacts. Waverly had >15 VERIFIED before L15 step 5, so the flag triggered and skipped 5 Serper discovery queries + 1 Claude extract

 + ion. Estimated 3-5 of the lost 13 contacts came from there.

 38 +

 39 + Both losses are legitimate — neither path can be recovered by a small patch to the existing flags. Round 1 in its current shape is incompatible with the 95% verified quality floor.

 40 +

 41 + **Round 1.1 options (require fresh planning, did NOT ship in S61):**

 42 +

 43 + 1. **Per-URL content merge:** instead of dedup, concatenate all distinct contents for a URL into one Claude call. Preserves all snippet text + full page content with one call instead of N. Requi

 + res careful token budgeting (20k char cap per extract_contacts call). Most promising, biggest scope change.

 44 + 2. **Raise L15 Step 5 threshold to 30+:** at threshold 30, Waverly's 30 verified wouldn't trigger the skip. But Conejo Valley / Cincinnati / Cypress-Fairbanks probably also land at 30-40 verifie

 + d, making the skip almost never fire — savings vanish.

 45 + 3. **Ship only usage logging, abandon URL dedup + L15 skip:** gives measurement-grade cost data for Round 2 planning but zero cost savings in Round 1. Honest minimum.

 46 + 4. **Different cost lever entirely:** batch L9 claude calls (5-10 pages per call instead of 1 per page). Round 2 scope but might be bringable forward.

 47 +

 48 + **Round 1 code state: ship as-is.** The flags default OFF so `agent/main.py`'s 4 call sites still produce byte-for-byte v1 behavior. The module-level usage capture is gated on `log_claude_usage=

 + True` — it's off unless the harness explicitly flips it. Test suite is 21/21 green. No production risk. The flags are available as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 49 +

 50 + **Budget burned in Session 61 A/B runs: ~$4.00** (Level 2 Waverly pre-merge check ~$1.50, A/B run 1 ~$1.21, A/B run 2 ~$1.25). Well under the $5 harness ceiling. Level 3 targets 2-5 (Lake Zurich

 + , Conejo Valley, Cincinnati, Cypress-Fairbanks) NOT executed — stopped on first failure per plan rule, saved ~$8.

 51 +

 52 + ### Session 61 lessons (most recent, load-bearing)

 53 +

 54 + - **`raw_pages` is not a page list — it's an extraction-request list.** Multiple entries per URL are intentional: different Serper queries produce distinct snippet highlighting for the same URL,

 + and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers add full-page versions of the same URL. Any dedup that collapses them to one entry loses information. This wasn't in the Round 1 plan's assumption set

 + because I looked at the dedup target without tracing the population pattern.

 55 + - **"Pure cost win with zero quality loss" is a claim that must be verified against real data, not asserted at plan time.** The Round 1 plan assumed URL dedup was lossless. It wasn't. The A/B ha

 + rness caught this on the very first target — which is exactly what the harness is for — but the plan's estimated impact was wrong by a meaningful margin.

 56 + - **95% is a strict floor, not a soft target.** The plan set verified_quality_gate at `v2 ≥ v1 × 0.95` (CLAUDE.md quality bar). Under that rule, losing 8 of 30 verified is a 27% loss — 5.4x the

 + allowed tolerance. No amount of cost savings compensates for that loss under Steven's "no sacrifice" quality rule.

 57 + - **Stop-on-first-failure is the right rule.** I saved ~$8 and ~60 min by not running Cincinnati / Conejo / Lake Zurich / Cypress-Fairbanks. The failure pattern is mechanical (Serper snippet los

 + s + L15 Step 5 skip), same root cause on every target. More data points would have confirmed the same finding, not revealed new ones.

 58 + - **Level 2 pre-merge checks catch pure regressions; Level 3 A/B checks catch design failures.** Level 2 (stash → run → unstash → diff) proved Part 0 was safe. Level 3 (two engines side-by-side

 + with real targets) proved Part 1 flags were unsafe as designed. Both matter — one is not a substitute for the other.

 59 + - **Ship with flags OFF by default. Failed flags stay dark.** The three Round 1 flags exist in `ResearchJob.__init__` but default to values that preserve v1 behavior. Production is unchanged. Th

 + e next session can iterate on flag behavior without having to revert and re-ship anything.

 60 +

 61 + ---

 62 +

 64 +

 ⏺ Gate failed — v2 lost 11 verified contacts (30→19). URL dedup is too aggressive. Let me check _add_raw_from_serper to understand the root cause.

 ⏺ Root cause found: _add_raw_from_serper appends each Serper hit with different snippet text per query, and other layers (L6 fetch, L18 Firecrawl, L16/L17 Exa) append the same URL with full-page content . My

 URL-only dedup keeps the first occurrence, often throwing away richer content. Fix: dedup by URL but keep the longest content for each URL.

 6 ## CURRENT STATE — update this after each session

 8 + **Session 61 shipped Research Engine Round 1 code across 4 commits. Level 3 Waverly A/B failed the verified_quality gate; Level 3 halted at target 1 per plan's "stop on first failure" rule. Flag

 + s default OFF so production is unchanged — v1 behavior is byte-for-byte preserved.**

 9 +

 10 + ### Session 61 commits (all on main)

 11 +

 12 + | commit | scope |

 13 + |---|---|

 14 + | `978499b` | Part 0: shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision helper in contact_extractor, rewire extract_from_multiple + ResearchJob._merge_contacts). Level 2 Waverly check: 37/37 contacts prese

 + rved, zero regression. 7 unit tests green. |

 15 + | `bd7a562` | Part 1: three feature flags on ResearchJob.__init__ (enable_url_dedup, l15_step5_skip_threshold, log_claude_usage). All default OFF. Module-level Claude usage capture in contact_ex

 + tractor.py is safe across run_in_executor thread boundaries because CPython GIL + serial ResearchQueue guarantee. 20 unit tests green. |

 16 + | `21d9b3b` | Part 2: A/B harness (scripts/ab_research_engine.py + ab_analyze.py). Measurement-grade cost from response.usage token counts. --max-cost-usd ceiling. 3 numerical gates. |

 17 + | `e62f4be` | Round 1.1 patch: URL dedup keeps longest content per URL (first-occurrence rule lost full-page content to short Serper snippets). 21 unit tests green. |

 18 +

 19 + ### Level 3 Waverly A/B — FAIL, stopped at target 1

 20 +

 21 + Two runs against "Waverly School District 145" NE:

 22 +

 23 + | metric | v1 baseline | v2 flags-on (first-wins) | v2 flags-on (longest-wins) |

 24 + |---|---|---|---|

 25 + | contacts total | 34 / 36 | 20 | 23 |

 26 + | contacts verified | **30 / 30** | **19** | **22** |

 27 + | claude calls | 134 / 137 | 65 | 69 |

 28 + | wall clock (s) | 334 / 329 | 171 | 179 |

 29 + | cost usd (measurement) | $0.80 / $0.81 | $0.41 | $0.44 |

 30 + | verified_quality_gate (≥95%) | — | **FAIL** (63%) | **FAIL** (73%) |

 31 + | cost_reduction_gate (≤90%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 32 + | wall_clock_gate (≤105%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 33 +

 34 + **Root cause of verified loss** (13 unique contacts in v1 that v2 missed):

 35 +

 36 + - **URL dedup is lossy for Serper snippets.** Each `_add_raw_from_serper` call appends per-query with different snippet text — the same URL gets 2-4 distinct snippet entries where each snippet h

 + ighlights sentences matching that query. Dedup by URL (either first-wins OR longest-wins) collapses those to one entry and loses the distinct per-query highlighting. Longest-wins recovered ~3 co

 + ntacts vs first-wins, but ~8 contacts remain lost.

 37 + - **L15 Step 5 skip at threshold=15** drops discovery search contacts. Waverly had >15 VERIFIED before L15 step 5, so the flag triggered and skipped 5 Serper discovery queries + 1 Claude extract

 + ion. Estimated 3-5 of the lost 13 contacts came from there.

 38 +

 39 + Both losses are legitimate — neither path can be recovered by a small patch to the existing flags. Round 1 in its current shape is incompatible with the 95% verified quality floor.

 40 +

 41 + **Round 1.1 options (require fresh planning, did NOT ship in S61):**

 42 +

 43 + 1. **Per-URL content merge:** instead of dedup, concatenate all distinct contents for a URL into one Claude call. Preserves all snippet text + full page content with one call instead of N. Requi

 + res careful token budgeting (20k char cap per extract_contacts call). Most promising, biggest scope change.

 44 + 2. **Raise L15 Step 5 threshold to 30+:** at threshold 30, Waverly's 30 verified wouldn't trigger the skip. But Conejo Valley / Cincinnati / Cypress-Fairbanks probably also land at 30-40 verifie

 + d, making the skip almost never fire — savings vanish.

 45 + 3. **Ship only usage logging, abandon URL dedup + L15 skip:** gives measurement-grade cost data for Round 2 planning but zero cost savings in Round 1. Honest minimum.

 46 + 4. **Different cost lever entirely:** batch L9 claude calls (5-10 pages per call instead of 1 per page). Round 2 scope but might be bringable forward.

 47 +

 48 + **Round 1 code state: ship as-is.** The flags default OFF so `agent/main.py`'s 4 call sites still produce byte-for-byte v1 behavior. The module-level usage capture is gated on `log_claude_usage=

 + True` — it's off unless the harness explicitly flips it. Test suite is 21/21 green. No production risk. The flags are available as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 49 +

 50 + **Budget burned in Session 61 A/B runs: ~$4.00** (Level 2 Waverly pre-merge check ~$1.50, A/B run 1 ~$1.21, A/B run 2 ~$1.25). Well under the $5 harness ceiling. Level 3 targets 2-5 (Lake Zurich

 + , Conejo Valley, Cincinnati, Cypress-Fairbanks) NOT executed — stopped on first failure per plan rule, saved ~$8.

 51 +

 52 + ### Session 61 lessons (most recent, load-bearing)

 53 +

 54 + - **`raw_pages` is not a page list — it's an extraction-request list.** Multiple entries per URL are intentional: different Serper queries produce distinct snippet highlighting for the same URL,

 + and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers add full-page versions of the same URL. Any dedup that collapses them to one entry loses information. This wasn't in the Round 1 plan's assumption set

 + because I looked at the dedup target without tracing the population pattern.

 55 + - **"Pure cost win with zero quality loss" is a claim that must be verified against real data, not asserted at plan time.** The Round 1 plan assumed URL dedup was lossless. It wasn't. The A/B ha

 + rness caught this on the very first target — which is exactly what the harness is for — but the plan's estimated impact was wrong by a meaningful margin.

 56 + - **95% is a strict floor, not a soft target.** The plan set verified_quality_gate at `v2 ≥ v1 × 0.95` (CLAUDE.md quality bar). Under that rule, losing 8 of 30 verified is a 27% loss — 5.4x the

 + allowed tolerance. No amount of cost savings compensates for that loss under Steven's "no sacrifice" quality rule.

 57 + - **Stop-on-first-failure is the right rule.** I saved ~$8 and ~60 min by not running Cincinnati / Conejo / Lake Zurich / Cypress-Fairbanks. The failure pattern is mechanical (Serper snippet los

 + s + L15 Step 5 skip), same root cause on every target. More data points would have confirmed the same finding, not revealed new ones.

 58 + - **Level 2 pre-merge checks catch pure regressions; Level 3 A/B checks catch design failures.** Level 2 (stash → run → unstash → diff) proved Part 0 was safe. Level 3 (two engines side-by-side

 + with real targets) proved Part 1 flags were unsafe as designed. Both matter — one is not a substitute for the other.

 59 + - **Ship with flags OFF by default. Failed flags stay dark.** The three Round 1 flags exist in `ResearchJob.__init__` but default to values that preserve v1 behavior. Production is unchanged. Th

 + e next session can iterate on flag behavior without having to revert and re-ship anything.

 1 # SCOUT — Claude Code Reference

 2 - *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — End of Session 60 (schedule ID map correction shipped; research engine Round 1 plan approved but implementation pending. 20 memory files banked.)*

 2 + *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — End of Session 61 (Research Engine Round 1 code shipped across 4 commits; Level 3 Waverly A/B failed verified_quality_gate; root cause documented; flags default OFF pr

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 57 lines

 6 ## CURRENT STATE — update this after each session

 8 + **Session 61 shipped Research Engine Round 1 code across 4 commits. Level 3 Waverly A/B failed the verified_quality gate; Level 3 halted at target 1 per plan's "stop on first failure" rule. Flag

 + s default OFF so production is unchanged — v1 behavior is byte-for-byte preserved.**

 9 +

 10 + ### Session 61 commits (all on main)

 11 +

 12 + | commit | scope |

 13 + |---|---|

 14 + | `978499b` | Part 0: shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision helper in contact_extractor, rewire extract_from_multiple + ResearchJob._merge_contacts). Level 2 Waverly check: 37/37 contacts prese

 + rved, zero regression. 7 unit tests green. |

 15 + | `bd7a562` | Part 1: three feature flags on ResearchJob.__init__ (enable_url_dedup, l15_step5_skip_threshold, log_claude_usage). All default OFF. Module-level Claude usage capture in contact_ex

 + tractor.py is safe across run_in_executor thread boundaries because CPython GIL + serial ResearchQueue guarantee. 20 unit tests green. |

 16 + | `21d9b3b` | Part 2: A/B harness (scripts/ab_research_engine.py + ab_analyze.py). Measurement-grade cost from response.usage token counts. --max-cost-usd ceiling. 3 numerical gates. |

 17 + | `e62f4be` | Round 1.1 patch: URL dedup keeps longest content per URL (first-occurrence rule lost full-page content to short Serper snippets). 21 unit tests green. |

 18 +

 19 + ### Level 3 Waverly A/B — FAIL, stopped at target 1

 20 +

 21 + Two runs against "Waverly School District 145" NE:

 22 +

 23 + | metric | v1 baseline | v2 flags-on (first-wins) | v2 flags-on (longest-wins) |

 24 + |---|---|---|---|

 25 + | contacts total | 34 / 36 | 20 | 23 |

 26 + | contacts verified | **30 / 30** | **19** | **22** |

 27 + | claude calls | 134 / 137 | 65 | 69 |

 28 + | wall clock (s) | 334 / 329 | 171 | 179 |

 29 + | cost usd (measurement) | $0.80 / $0.81 | $0.41 | $0.44 |

 30 + | verified_quality_gate (≥95%) | — | **FAIL** (63%) | **FAIL** (73%) |

 31 + | cost_reduction_gate (≤90%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 32 + | wall_clock_gate (≤105%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 33 +

 34 + **Root cause of verified loss** (13 unique contacts in v1 that v2 missed):

 35 +

 36 + - **URL dedup is lossy for Serper snippets.** Each `_add_raw_from_serper` call appends per-query with different snippet text — the same URL gets 2-4 distinct snippet entries where each snippet h

 + ighlights sentences matching that query. Dedup by URL (either first-wins OR longest-wins) collapses those to one entry and loses the distinct per-query highlighting. Longest-wins recovered ~3 co

 + ntacts vs first-wins, but ~8 contacts remain lost.

 37 + - **L15 Step 5 skip at threshold=15** drops discovery search contacts. Waverly had >15 VERIFIED before L15 step 5, so the flag triggered and skipped 5 Serper discovery queries + 1 Claude extract

 + ion. Estimated 3-5 of the lost 13 contacts came from there.

 38 +

 39 + Both losses are legitimate — neither path can be recovered by a small patch to the existing flags. Round 1 in its current shape is incompatible with the 95% verified quality floor.

 40 +

 41 + **Round 1.1 options (require fresh planning, did NOT ship in S61):**

 42 +

 43 + 1. **Per-URL content merge:** instead of dedup, concatenate all distinct contents for a URL into one Claude call. Preserves all snippet text + full page content with one call instead of N. Requi

 + res careful token budgeting (20k char cap per extract_contacts call). Most promising, biggest scope change.

 44 + 2. **Raise L15 Step 5 threshold to 30+:** at threshold 30, Waverly's 30 verified wouldn't trigger the skip. But Conejo Valley / Cincinnati / Cypress-Fairbanks probably also land at 30-40 verifie

 + d, making the skip almost never fire — savings vanish.

 45 + 3. **Ship only usage logging, abandon URL dedup + L15 skip:** gives measurement-grade cost data for Round 2 planning but zero cost savings in Round 1. Honest minimum.

 46 + 4. **Different cost lever entirely:** batch L9 claude calls (5-10 pages per call instead of 1 per page). Round 2 scope but might be bringable forward.

 47 +

 48 + **Round 1 code state: ship as-is.** The flags default OFF so `agent/main.py`'s 4 call sites still produce byte-for-byte v1 behavior. The module-level usage capture is gated on `log_claude_usage=

 + True` — it's off unless the harness explicitly flips it. Test suite is 21/21 green. No production risk. The flags are available as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 49 +

 50 + **Budget burned in Session 61 A/B runs: ~$4.00** (Level 2 Waverly pre-merge check ~$1.50, A/B run 1 ~$1.21, A/B run 2 ~$1.25). Well under the $5 harness ceiling. Level 3 targets 2-5 (Lake Zurich

 + , Conejo Valley, Cincinnati, Cypress-Fairbanks) NOT executed — stopped on first failure per plan rule, saved ~$8.

 51 +

 52 + ### Session 61 lessons (most recent, load-bearing)

 53 +

 54 + - **`raw_pages` is not a page list — it's an extraction-request list.** Multiple entries per URL are intentional: different Serper queries produce distinct snippet highlighting for the same URL,

 + and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers add full-page versions of the same URL. Any dedup that collapses them to one entry loses information. This wasn't in the Round 1 plan's assumption set

 + because I looked at the dedup target without tracing the population pattern.

 55 + - **"Pure cost win with zero quality loss" is a claim that must be verified against real data, not asserted at plan time.** The Round 1 plan assumed URL dedup was lossless. It wasn't. The A/B ha

 + rness caught this on the very first target — which is exactly what the harness is for — but the plan's estimated impact was wrong by a meaningful margin.

 56 + - **95% is a strict floor, not a soft target.** The plan set verified_quality_gate at `v2 ≥ v1 × 0.95` (CLAUDE.md quality bar). Under that rule, losing 8 of 30 verified is a 27% loss — 5.4x the

 + allowed tolerance. No amount of cost savings compensates for that loss under Steven's "no sacrifice" quality rule.

 57 + - **Stop-on-first-failure is the right rule.** I saved ~$8 and ~60 min by not running Cincinnati / Conejo / Lake Zurich / Cypress-Fairbanks. The failure pattern is mechanical (Serper snippet los

 + s + L15 Step 5 skip), same root cause on every target. More data points would have confirmed the same finding, not revealed new ones.

 58 + - **Level 2 pre-merge checks catch pure regressions; Level 3 A/B checks catch design failures.** Level 2 (stash → run → unstash → diff) proved Part 0 was safe. Level 3 (two engines side-by-side

 + with real targets) proved Part 1 flags were unsafe as designed. Both matter — one is not a substitute for the other.

 59 + - **Ship with flags OFF by default. Failed flags stay dark.** The three Round 1 flags exist in `ResearchJob.__init__` but default to values that preserve v1 behavior. Production is unchanged. Th

 + e next session can iterate on flag behavior without having to revert and re-ship anything.

 60 +

 61 + ---

 62 +

 63 + ## SESSION 60 HISTORY (kept for continuity)

 64 +

 65 **Session 60 ended mid-execution at context limit.** Two distinct pieces of work:

 67 ### 1. Schedule ID map correction — SHIPPED (commit `846aaed`)

 - Round 1 code is shipped on main across 5 commits. Flags default OFF so production (your 4 ResearchJob call sites in agent/main.py ) is byte-for-byte v1 — nothing changed for you.

 2/3 the data they need. ~20 min, no API spend.

 shared-city gap + F9 scanner redesign are also on the carryover list. Any of those are useful non-research-engine work.

 ⏺ Gate failed — v2 lost 11 verified contacts (30→19). URL dedup is too aggressive. Let me check _add_raw_from_serper to understand the root cause.

 ⏺ Root cause found: _add_raw_from_serper appends each Serper hit with different snippet text per query, and other layers (L6 fetch, L18 Firecrawl, L16/L17 Exa) append the same URL with full-page content . My

 URL-only dedup keeps the first occurrence, often throwing away richer content. Fix: dedup by URL but keep the longest content for each URL.

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 57 lines

 6 ## CURRENT STATE — update this after each session

 8 + **Session 61 shipped Research Engine Round 1 code across 4 commits. Level 3 Waverly A/B failed the verified_quality gate; Level 3 halted at target 1 per plan's "stop on first failure" rule. Flag

 + s default OFF so production is unchanged — v1 behavior is byte-for-byte preserved.**

 9 +

 10 + ### Session 61 commits (all on main)

 11 +

 12 + | commit | scope |

 13 + |---|---|

 14 + | `978499b` | Part 0: shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision helper in contact_extractor, rewire extract_from_multiple + ResearchJob._merge_contacts). Level 2 Waverly check: 37/37 contacts prese

 + rved, zero regression. 7 unit tests green. |

 15 + | `bd7a562` | Part 1: three feature flags on ResearchJob.__init__ (enable_url_dedup, l15_step5_skip_threshold, log_claude_usage). All default OFF. Module-level Claude usage capture in contact_ex

 + tractor.py is safe across run_in_executor thread boundaries because CPython GIL + serial ResearchQueue guarantee. 20 unit tests green. |

 16 + | `21d9b3b` | Part 2: A/B harness (scripts/ab_research_engine.py + ab_analyze.py). Measurement-grade cost from response.usage token counts. --max-cost-usd ceiling. 3 numerical gates. |

 17 + | `e62f4be` | Round 1.1 patch: URL dedup keeps longest content per URL (first-occurrence rule lost full-page content to short Serper snippets). 21 unit tests green. |

 18 +

 19 + ### Level 3 Waverly A/B — FAIL, stopped at target 1

 20 +

 21 + Two runs against "Waverly School District 145" NE:

 22 +

 23 + | metric | v1 baseline | v2 flags-on (first-wins) | v2 flags-on (longest-wins) |

 24 + |---|---|---|---|

 25 + | contacts total | 34 / 36 | 20 | 23 |

 26 + | contacts verified | **30 / 30** | **19** | **22** |

 27 + | claude calls | 134 / 137 | 65 | 69 |

 28 + | wall clock (s) | 334 / 329 | 171 | 179 |

 29 + | cost usd (measurement) | $0.80 / $0.81 | $0.41 | $0.44 |

 30 + | verified_quality_gate (≥95%) | — | **FAIL** (63%) | **FAIL** (73%) |

 31 + | cost_reduction_gate (≤90%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 32 + | wall_clock_gate (≤105%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 33 +

 34 + **Root cause of verified loss** (13 unique contacts in v1 that v2 missed):

 35 +

 36 + - **URL dedup is lossy for Serper snippets.** Each `_add_raw_from_serper` call appends per-query with different snippet text — the same URL gets 2-4 distinct snippet entries where each snippet h

 + ighlights sentences matching that query. Dedup by URL (either first-wins OR longest-wins) collapses those to one entry and loses the distinct per-query highlighting. Longest-wins recovered ~3 co

 + ntacts vs first-wins, but ~8 contacts remain lost.

 37 + - **L15 Step 5 skip at threshold=15** drops discovery search contacts. Waverly had >15 VERIFIED before L15 step 5, so the flag triggered and skipped 5 Serper discovery queries + 1 Claude extract

 + ion. Estimated 3-5 of the lost 13 contacts came from there.

 38 +

 39 + Both losses are legitimate — neither path can be recovered by a small patch to the existing flags. Round 1 in its current shape is incompatible with the 95% verified quality floor.

 40 +

 41 + **Round 1.1 options (require fresh planning, did NOT ship in S61):**

 42 +

 43 + 1. **Per-URL content merge:** instead of dedup, concatenate all distinct contents for a URL into one Claude call. Preserves all snippet text + full page content with one call instead of N. Requi

 + res careful token budgeting (20k char cap per extract_contacts call). Most promising, biggest scope change.

 44 + 2. **Raise L15 Step 5 threshold to 30+:** at threshold 30, Waverly's 30 verified wouldn't trigger the skip. But Conejo Valley / Cincinnati / Cypress-Fairbanks probably also land at 30-40 verifie

 + d, making the skip almost never fire — savings vanish.

 45 + 3. **Ship only usage logging, abandon URL dedup + L15 skip:** gives measurement-grade cost data for Round 2 planning but zero cost savings in Round 1. Honest minimum.

 46 + 4. **Different cost lever entirely:** batch L9 claude calls (5-10 pages per call instead of 1 per page). Round 2 scope but might be bringable forward.

 47 +

 48 + **Round 1 code state: ship as-is.** The flags default OFF so `agent/main.py`'s 4 call sites still produce byte-for-byte v1 behavior. The module-level usage capture is gated on `log_claude_usage=

 + True` — it's off unless the harness explicitly flips it. Test suite is 21/21 green. No production risk. The flags are available as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 49 +

 50 + **Budget burned in Session 61 A/B runs: ~$4.00** (Level 2 Waverly pre-merge check ~$1.50, A/B run 1 ~$1.21, A/B run 2 ~$1.25). Well under the $5 harness ceiling. Level 3 targets 2-5 (Lake Zurich

 + , Conejo Valley, Cincinnati, Cypress-Fairbanks) NOT executed — stopped on first failure per plan rule, saved ~$8.

 51 +

 52 + ### Session 61 lessons (most recent, load-bearing)

 53 +

 54 + - **`raw_pages` is not a page list — it's an extraction-request list.** Multiple entries per URL are intentional: different Serper queries produce distinct snippet highlighting for the same URL,

 + and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers add full-page versions of the same URL. Any dedup that collapses them to one entry loses information. This wasn't in the Round 1 plan's assumption set

 + because I looked at the dedup target without tracing the population pattern.

 55 + - **"Pure cost win with zero quality loss" is a claim that must be verified against real data, not asserted at plan time.** The Round 1 plan assumed URL dedup was lossless. It wasn't. The A/B ha

 + rness caught this on the very first target — which is exactly what the harness is for — but the plan's estimated impact was wrong by a meaningful margin.

 56 + - **95% is a strict floor, not a soft target.** The plan set verified_quality_gate at `v2 ≥ v1 × 0.95` (CLAUDE.md quality bar). Under that rule, losing 8 of 30 verified is a 27% loss — 5.4x the

 + allowed tolerance. No amount of cost savings compensates for that loss under Steven's "no sacrifice" quality rule.

 57 + - **Stop-on-first-failure is the right rule.** I saved ~$8 and ~60 min by not running Cincinnati / Conejo / Lake Zurich / Cypress-Fairbanks. The failure pattern is mechanical (Serper snippet los

 + s + L15 Step 5 skip), same root cause on every target. More data points would have confirmed the same finding, not revealed new ones.

 58 + - **Level 2 pre-merge checks catch pure regressions; Level 3 A/B checks catch design failures.** Level 2 (stash → run → unstash → diff) proved Part 0 was safe. Level 3 (two engines side-by-side

 + with real targets) proved Part 1 flags were unsafe as designed. Both matter — one is not a substitute for the other.

 59 + - **Ship with flags OFF by default. Failed flags stay dark.** The three Round 1 flags exist in `ResearchJob.__init__` but default to values that preserve v1 behavior. Production is unchanged. Th

 + e next session can iterate on flag behavior without having to revert and re-ship anything.

 60 +

 61 + ---

 62 +

 63 + ## SESSION 60 HISTORY (kept for continuity)

 64 +

 65 **Session 60 ended mid-execution at context limit.** Two distinct pieces of work:

 67 ### 1. Schedule ID map correction — SHIPPED (commit `846aaed`)

 ⏺ Sorry — I finished the execution and wrapped up without handing control back cleanly. There's no active question. Here's where we are and the options:

 Where we stand:

 - Round 1 code is shipped on main across 5 commits. Flags default OFF so production (your 4 ResearchJob call sites in agent/main.py ) is byte-for-byte v1 — nothing changed for you.

 - The A/B harness is built and working. It caught a real quality regression on the very first target (Waverly), which is exactly what it's for.

 - Round 1 as designed cannot pass your 95% verified-quality floor. URL dedup loses per-query Serper snippet highlighting; L15 Step 5 skip drops discovery contacts. Both losses are legitimate.

 - ~$4 burned of your $25/week research budget. I stopped after Waverly instead of burning ~$8 more on 4 more targets that would fail the same way.

 Three things we could do next — your call:

 1. Plan Round 1.1 properly. The most promising direction is per-URL content MERGE (concatenate distinct pieces per URL into one Claude call) instead of dedup. Needs a fresh pressure-tested plan before any

 code. ~30-60 min of plan-mode work, no API spend.

 2. Ship only the measurement flag + abandon the cost flags. Keep log_claude_usage (measurement-grade cost instrument for all future rounds), turn off the other two. Zero cost savings in Round 1 but gives Round

 2/3 the data they need. ~20 min, no API spend.

 3. Park research engine, pick up Session 60 carryover instead. The 6 diocesan sequences (IDs 2008-2013) are still sitting waiting for you to activate in the Outreach UI. The 9 pending dioceses + BUG 5

 shared-city gap + F9 scanner redesign are also on the carryover list. Any of those are useful non-research-engine work.

 What do you want to do?

 ⏺ Gate failed — v2 lost 11 verified contacts (30→19). URL dedup is too aggressive. Let me check _add_raw_from_serper to understand the root cause.

 ⏺ Root cause found: _add_raw_from_serper appends each Serper hit with different snippet text per query, and other layers (L6 fetch, L18 Firecrawl, L16/L17 Exa) append the same URL with full-page content . My

 URL-only dedup keeps the first occurrence, often throwing away richer content. Fix: dedup by URL but keep the longest content for each URL.

 2 - *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — End of Session 60 (schedule ID map correction shipped; research engine Round 1 plan approved but implementation pending. 20 memory files banked.)*

 2 + *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — End of Session 61 (Research Engine Round 1 code shipped across 4 commits; Level 3 Waverly A/B failed verified_quality_gate; root cause documented; flags default OFF pr

 + eserves v1 production behavior; Round 1.1 planning needed before the flags can flip on.)*

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 57 lines

 6 ## CURRENT STATE — update this after each session

 8 + **Session 61 shipped Research Engine Round 1 code across 4 commits. Level 3 Waverly A/B failed the verified_quality gate; Level 3 halted at target 1 per plan's "stop on first failure" rule. Flag

 + s default OFF so production is unchanged — v1 behavior is byte-for-byte preserved.**

 9 +

 10 + ### Session 61 commits (all on main)

 11 +

 12 + | commit | scope |

 13 + |---|---|

 14 + | `978499b` | Part 0: shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision helper in contact_extractor, rewire extract_from_multiple + ResearchJob._merge_contacts). Level 2 Waverly check: 37/37 contacts prese

 + rved, zero regression. 7 unit tests green. |

 15 + | `bd7a562` | Part 1: three feature flags on ResearchJob.__init__ (enable_url_dedup, l15_step5_skip_threshold, log_claude_usage). All default OFF. Module-level Claude usage capture in contact_ex

 + tractor.py is safe across run_in_executor thread boundaries because CPython GIL + serial ResearchQueue guarantee. 20 unit tests green. |

 16 + | `21d9b3b` | Part 2: A/B harness (scripts/ab_research_engine.py + ab_analyze.py). Measurement-grade cost from response.usage token counts. --max-cost-usd ceiling. 3 numerical gates. |

 17 + | `e62f4be` | Round 1.1 patch: URL dedup keeps longest content per URL (first-occurrence rule lost full-page content to short Serper snippets). 21 unit tests green. |

 18 +

 19 + ### Level 3 Waverly A/B — FAIL, stopped at target 1

 20 +

 21 + Two runs against "Waverly School District 145" NE:

 22 +

 23 + | metric | v1 baseline | v2 flags-on (first-wins) | v2 flags-on (longest-wins) |

 24 + |---|---|---|---|

 25 + | contacts total | 34 / 36 | 20 | 23 |

 26 + | contacts verified | **30 / 30** | **19** | **22** |

 27 + | claude calls | 134 / 137 | 65 | 69 |

 28 + | wall clock (s) | 334 / 329 | 171 | 179 |

 29 + | cost usd (measurement) | $0.80 / $0.81 | $0.41 | $0.44 |

 30 + | verified_quality_gate (≥95%) | — | **FAIL** (63%) | **FAIL** (73%) |

 31 + | cost_reduction_gate (≤90%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 32 + | wall_clock_gate (≤105%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 33 +

 34 + **Root cause of verified loss** (13 unique contacts in v1 that v2 missed):

 35 +

 36 + - **URL dedup is lossy for Serper snippets.** Each `_add_raw_from_serper` call appends per-query with different snippet text — the same URL gets 2-4 distinct snippet entries where each snippet h

 + ighlights sentences matching that query. Dedup by URL (either first-wins OR longest-wins) collapses those to one entry and loses the distinct per-query highlighting. Longest-wins recovered ~3 co

 + ntacts vs first-wins, but ~8 contacts remain lost.

 37 + - **L15 Step 5 skip at threshold=15** drops discovery search contacts. Waverly had >15 VERIFIED before L15 step 5, so the flag triggered and skipped 5 Serper discovery queries + 1 Claude extract

 + ion. Estimated 3-5 of the lost 13 contacts came from there.

 38 +

 39 + Both losses are legitimate — neither path can be recovered by a small patch to the existing flags. Round 1 in its current shape is incompatible with the 95% verified quality floor.

 40 +

 41 + **Round 1.1 options (require fresh planning, did NOT ship in S61):**

 42 +

 43 + 1. **Per-URL content merge:** instead of dedup, concatenate all distinct contents for a URL into one Claude call. Preserves all snippet text + full page content with one call instead of N. Requi

 + res careful token budgeting (20k char cap per extract_contacts call). Most promising, biggest scope change.

 44 + 2. **Raise L15 Step 5 threshold to 30+:** at threshold 30, Waverly's 30 verified wouldn't trigger the skip. But Conejo Valley / Cincinnati / Cypress-Fairbanks probably also land at 30-40 verifie

 + d, making the skip almost never fire — savings vanish.

 45 + 3. **Ship only usage logging, abandon URL dedup + L15 skip:** gives measurement-grade cost data for Round 2 planning but zero cost savings in Round 1. Honest minimum.

 46 + 4. **Different cost lever entirely:** batch L9 claude calls (5-10 pages per call instead of 1 per page). Round 2 scope but might be bringable forward.

 47 +

 48 + **Round 1 code state: ship as-is.** The flags default OFF so `agent/main.py`'s 4 call sites still produce byte-for-byte v1 behavior. The module-level usage capture is gated on `log_claude_usage=

 + True` — it's off unless the harness explicitly flips it. Test suite is 21/21 green. No production risk. The flags are available as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 49 +

 50 + **Budget burned in Session 61 A/B runs: ~$4.00** (Level 2 Waverly pre-merge check ~$1.50, A/B run 1 ~$1.21, A/B run 2 ~$1.25). Well under the $5 harness ceiling. Level 3 targets 2-5 (Lake Zurich

 + , Conejo Valley, Cincinnati, Cypress-Fairbanks) NOT executed — stopped on first failure per plan rule, saved ~$8.

 51 +

 52 + ### Session 61 lessons (most recent, load-bearing)

 53 +

 54 + - **`raw_pages` is not a page list — it's an extraction-request list.** Multiple entries per URL are intentional: different Serper queries produce distinct snippet highlighting for the same URL,

 + and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers add full-page versions of the same URL. Any dedup that collapses them to one entry loses information. This wasn't in the Round 1 plan's assumption set

 + because I looked at the dedup target without tracing the population pattern.

 55 + - **"Pure cost win with zero quality loss" is a claim that must be verified against real data, not asserted at plan time.** The Round 1 plan assumed URL dedup was lossless. It wasn't. The A/B ha

 + rness caught this on the very first target — which is exactly what the harness is for — but the plan's estimated impact was wrong by a meaningful margin.

 56 + - **95% is a strict floor, not a soft target.** The plan set verified_quality_gate at `v2 ≥ v1 × 0.95` (CLAUDE.md quality bar). Under that rule, losing 8 of 30 verified is a 27% loss — 5.4x the

 + allowed tolerance. No amount of cost savings compensates for that loss under Steven's "no sacrifice" quality rule.

 57 + - **Stop-on-first-failure is the right rule.** I saved ~$8 and ~60 min by not running Cincinnati / Conejo / Lake Zurich / Cypress-Fairbanks. The failure pattern is mechanical (Serper snippet los

 + s + L15 Step 5 skip), same root cause on every target. More data points would have confirmed the same finding, not revealed new ones.

 58 + - **Level 2 pre-merge checks catch pure regressions; Level 3 A/B checks catch design failures.** Level 2 (stash → run → unstash → diff) proved Part 0 was safe. Level 3 (two engines side-by-side

 + with real targets) proved Part 1 flags were unsafe as designed. Both matter — one is not a substitute for the other.

 59 + - **Ship with flags OFF by default. Failed flags stay dark.** The three Round 1 flags exist in `ResearchJob.__init__` but default to values that preserve v1 behavior. Production is unchanged. Th

 + e next session can iterate on flag behavior without having to revert and re-ship anything.

 60 +

 61 + ---

 62 +

 63 + ## SESSION 60 HISTORY (kept for continuity)

 64 +

 65 **Session 60 ended mid-execution at context limit.** Two distinct pieces of work:

 67 ### 1. Schedule ID map correction — SHIPPED (commit `846aaed`)

 ⏺ Session 61 done. Five commits shipped on main :

 - 978499b Part 0 shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision, 7 tests, Level 2 Waverly check green)

 - bd7a562 Part 1 three feature flags (all default OFF, 20 tests green)

 - 21d9b3b Part 2 A/B harness with real response.usage cost tracking

 - e62f4be Round 1.1 longest-content-wins patch after first A/B exposed a regression

 - 163fc8f CLAUDE.md session wrap with full root cause + Round 1.1 options

 Level 3 validation stopped at Waverly on verified_quality_gate failure: v1=30 verified, v2=22 (73% of baseline, below the 95% floor). Root cause: URL dedup collapses distinct per-query Serper snippet

 highlighting, and L15 Step 5 skip drops discovery contacts. Both losses are legitimate — not fixable by small patches. Round 1.1 needs fresh planning (per-URL content MERGE, not dedup, is the most promising

 direction).

 Zero production risk. All three flags default OFF; production's 4 ResearchJob call sites continue to get byte-for-byte v1 behavior. Flags remain as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 feedback_raw_pages_is_extraction_requests.md .

 ⏺ Sorry — I finished the execution and wrapped up without handing control back cleanly. There's no active question. Here's where we are and the options:

 Where we stand:

 - Round 1 code is shipped on main across 5 commits. Flags default OFF so production (your 4 ResearchJob call sites in agent/main.py ) is byte-for-byte v1 — nothing changed for you.

 - The A/B harness is built and working. It caught a real quality regression on the very first target (Waverly), which is exactly what it's for.

 - Round 1 as designed cannot pass your 95% verified-quality floor. URL dedup loses per-query Serper snippet highlighting; L15 Step 5 skip drops discovery contacts. Both losses are legitimate.

 - ~$4 burned of your $25/week research budget. I stopped after Waverly instead of burning ~$8 more on 4 more targets that would fail the same way.

 Three things we could do next — your call:

 1. Plan Round 1.1 properly. The most promising direction is per-URL content MERGE (concatenate distinct pieces per URL into one Claude call) instead of dedup. Needs a fresh pressure-tested plan before any

 code. ~30-60 min of plan-mode work, no API spend.

 2. Ship only the measurement flag + abandon the cost flags. Keep log_claude_usage (measurement-grade cost instrument for all future rounds), turn off the other two. Zero cost savings in Round 1 but gives Round

 2/3 the data they need. ~20 min, no API spend.

 3. Park research engine, pick up Session 60 carryover instead. The 6 diocesan sequences (IDs 2008-2013) are still sitting waiting for you to activate in the Outreach UI. The 9 pending dioceses + BUG 5

 shared-city gap + F9 scanner redesign are also on the carryover list. Any of those are useful non-research-engine work.

 What do you want to do?

 ⏺ Gate failed — v2 lost 11 verified contacts (30→19). URL dedup is too aggressive. Let me check _add_raw_from_serper to understand the root cause.

 ⏺ Root cause found: _add_raw_from_serper appends each Serper hit with different snippet text per query, and other layers (L6 fetch, L18 Firecrawl, L16/L17 Exa) append the same URL with full-page content . My

 URL-only dedup keeps the first occurrence, often throwing away richer content. Fix: dedup by URL but keep the longest content for each URL.

 ⏺ Update ( tools/research_engine.py )

 692 + # Round 1 Flag A: URL dedup with longest-content-wins.

 693 + # Serper snippets get appended per-query with different snippet text

 694 + # for the same URL, and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers append

 695 + # the same URL with full-page HTML. Naive first-occurrence dedup keeps

 696 + # the short snippet and throws away the full page — a quality loss.

 1 # SCOUT — Claude Code Reference

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 57 lines

 6 ## CURRENT STATE — update this after each session

 8 + **Session 61 shipped Research Engine Round 1 code across 4 commits. Level 3 Waverly A/B failed the verified_quality gate; Level 3 halted at target 1 per plan's "stop on first failure" rule. Flag

 + s default OFF so production is unchanged — v1 behavior is byte-for-byte preserved.**

 9 +

 10 + ### Session 61 commits (all on main)

 11 +

 12 + | commit | scope |

 13 + |---|---|

 14 + | `978499b` | Part 0: shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision helper in contact_extractor, rewire extract_from_multiple + ResearchJob._merge_contacts). Level 2 Waverly check: 37/37 contacts prese

 + rved, zero regression. 7 unit tests green. |

 15 + | `bd7a562` | Part 1: three feature flags on ResearchJob.__init__ (enable_url_dedup, l15_step5_skip_threshold, log_claude_usage). All default OFF. Module-level Claude usage capture in contact_ex

 + tractor.py is safe across run_in_executor thread boundaries because CPython GIL + serial ResearchQueue guarantee. 20 unit tests green. |

 16 + | `21d9b3b` | Part 2: A/B harness (scripts/ab_research_engine.py + ab_analyze.py). Measurement-grade cost from response.usage token counts. --max-cost-usd ceiling. 3 numerical gates. |

 17 + | `e62f4be` | Round 1.1 patch: URL dedup keeps longest content per URL (first-occurrence rule lost full-page content to short Serper snippets). 21 unit tests green. |

 18 +

 19 + ### Level 3 Waverly A/B — FAIL, stopped at target 1

 20 +

 21 + Two runs against "Waverly School District 145" NE:

 22 +

 23 + | metric | v1 baseline | v2 flags-on (first-wins) | v2 flags-on (longest-wins) |

 24 + |---|---|---|---|

 25 + | contacts total | 34 / 36 | 20 | 23 |

 26 + | contacts verified | **30 / 30** | **19** | **22** |

 27 + | claude calls | 134 / 137 | 65 | 69 |

 28 + | wall clock (s) | 334 / 329 | 171 | 179 |

 29 + | cost usd (measurement) | $0.80 / $0.81 | $0.41 | $0.44 |

 30 + | verified_quality_gate (≥95%) | — | **FAIL** (63%) | **FAIL** (73%) |

 31 + | cost_reduction_gate (≤90%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 32 + | wall_clock_gate (≤105%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 33 +

 34 + **Root cause of verified loss** (13 unique contacts in v1 that v2 missed):

 35 +

 36 + - **URL dedup is lossy for Serper snippets.** Each `_add_raw_from_serper` call appends per-query with different snippet text — the same URL gets 2-4 distinct snippet entries where each snippet h

 + ighlights sentences matching that query. Dedup by URL (either first-wins OR longest-wins) collapses those to one entry and loses the distinct per-query highlighting. Longest-wins recovered ~3 co

 + ntacts vs first-wins, but ~8 contacts remain lost.

 37 + - **L15 Step 5 skip at threshold=15** drops discovery search contacts. Waverly had >15 VERIFIED before L15 step 5, so the flag triggered and skipped 5 Serper discovery queries + 1 Claude extract

 + ion. Estimated 3-5 of the lost 13 contacts came from there.

 38 +

 39 + Both losses are legitimate — neither path can be recovered by a small patch to the existing flags. Round 1 in its current shape is incompatible with the 95% verified quality floor.

 40 +

 41 + **Round 1.1 options (require fresh planning, did NOT ship in S61):**

 42 +

 43 + 1. **Per-URL content merge:** instead of dedup, concatenate all distinct contents for a URL into one Claude call. Preserves all snippet text + full page content with one call instead of N. Requi

 + res careful token budgeting (20k char cap per extract_contacts call). Most promising, biggest scope change.

 44 + 2. **Raise L15 Step 5 threshold to 30+:** at threshold 30, Waverly's 30 verified wouldn't trigger the skip. But Conejo Valley / Cincinnati / Cypress-Fairbanks probably also land at 30-40 verifie

 + d, making the skip almost never fire — savings vanish.

 45 + 3. **Ship only usage logging, abandon URL dedup + L15 skip:** gives measurement-grade cost data for Round 2 planning but zero cost savings in Round 1. Honest minimum.

 46 + 4. **Different cost lever entirely:** batch L9 claude calls (5-10 pages per call instead of 1 per page). Round 2 scope but might be bringable forward.

 47 +

 48 + **Round 1 code state: ship as-is.** The flags default OFF so `agent/main.py`'s 4 call sites still produce byte-for-byte v1 behavior. The module-level usage capture is gated on `log_claude_usage=

 + True` — it's off unless the harness explicitly flips it. Test suite is 21/21 green. No production risk. The flags are available as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 49 +

 50 + **Budget burned in Session 61 A/B runs: ~$4.00** (Level 2 Waverly pre-merge check ~$1.50, A/B run 1 ~$1.21, A/B run 2 ~$1.25). Well under the $5 harness ceiling. Level 3 targets 2-5 (Lake Zurich

 + , Conejo Valley, Cincinnati, Cypress-Fairbanks) NOT executed — stopped on first failure per plan rule, saved ~$8.

 51 +

 52 + ### Session 61 lessons (most recent, load-bearing)

 53 +

 54 + - **`raw_pages` is not a page list — it's an extraction-request list.** Multiple entries per URL are intentional: different Serper queries produce distinct snippet highlighting for the same URL,

 + and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers add full-page versions of the same URL. Any dedup that collapses them to one entry loses information. This wasn't in the Round 1 plan's assumption set

 + because I looked at the dedup target without tracing the population pattern.

 55 + - **"Pure cost win with zero quality loss" is a claim that must be verified against real data, not asserted at plan time.** The Round 1 plan assumed URL dedup was lossless. It wasn't. The A/B ha

 + rness caught this on the very first target — which is exactly what the harness is for — but the plan's estimated impact was wrong by a meaningful margin.

 56 + - **95% is a strict floor, not a soft target.** The plan set verified_quality_gate at `v2 ≥ v1 × 0.95` (CLAUDE.md quality bar). Under that rule, losing 8 of 30 verified is a 27% loss — 5.4x the

 + allowed tolerance. No amount of cost savings compensates for that loss under Steven's "no sacrifice" quality rule.

 57 + - **Stop-on-first-failure is the right rule.** I saved ~$8 and ~60 min by not running Cincinnati / Conejo / Lake Zurich / Cypress-Fairbanks. The failure pattern is mechanical (Serper snippet los

 + s + L15 Step 5 skip), same root cause on every target. More data points would have confirmed the same finding, not revealed new ones.

 58 + - **Level 2 pre-merge checks catch pure regressions; Level 3 A/B checks catch design failures.** Level 2 (stash → run → unstash → diff) proved Part 0 was safe. Level 3 (two engines side-by-side

 + with real targets) proved Part 1 flags were unsafe as designed. Both matter — one is not a substitute for the other.

 59 + - **Ship with flags OFF by default. Failed flags stay dark.** The three Round 1 flags exist in `ResearchJob.__init__` but default to values that preserve v1 behavior. Production is unchanged. Th

 + e next session can iterate on flag behavior without having to revert and re-ship anything.

 60 +

 61 + ---

 62 +

 63 + ## SESSION 60 HISTORY (kept for continuity)

 64 +

 65 **Session 60 ended mid-execution at context limit.** Two distinct pieces of work:

 67 ### 1. Schedule ID map correction — SHIPPED (commit `846aaed`)

 ⏺ Sorry — I finished the execution and wrapped up without handing control back cleanly. There's no active question. Here's where we are and the options:

 Where we stand:

 - Round 1 code is shipped on main across 5 commits. Flags default OFF so production (your 4 ResearchJob call sites in agent/main.py ) is byte-for-byte v1 — nothing changed for you.

 - The A/B harness is built and working. It caught a real quality regression on the very first target (Waverly), which is exactly what it's for.

 - Round 1 as designed cannot pass your 95% verified-quality floor. URL dedup loses per-query Serper snippet highlighting; L15 Step 5 skip drops discovery contacts. Both losses are legitimate.

 - ~$4 burned of your $25/week research budget. I stopped after Waverly instead of burning ~$8 more on 4 more targets that would fail the same way.

 Three things we could do next — your call:

 1. Plan Round 1.1 properly. The most promising direction is per-URL content MERGE (concatenate distinct pieces per URL into one Claude call) instead of dedup. Needs a fresh pressure-tested plan before any

 code. ~30-60 min of plan-mode work, no API spend.

 2. Ship only the measurement flag + abandon the cost flags. Keep log_claude_usage (measurement-grade cost instrument for all future rounds), turn off the other two. Zero cost savings in Round 1 but gives Round

 2/3 the data they need. ~20 min, no API spend.

 3. Park research engine, pick up Session 60 carryover instead. The 6 diocesan sequences (IDs 2008-2013) are still sitting waiting for you to activate in the Outreach UI. The 9 pending dioceses + BUG 5

 shared-city gap + F9 scanner redesign are also on the carryover list. Any of those are useful non-research-engine work.

 What do you want to do?

 168 def test_url_dedup_exact_duplicate ():

 169 + # When two pages share a normalized URL, keep the one with LONGER content

 170 + # (preserves full fetched page over snippet).

 172 + job.raw_pages = [

 175 + ]

 1 # SCOUT — Claude Code Reference

 2 - *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — End of Session 60 (schedule ID map correction shipped; research engine Round 1 plan approved but implementation pending. 20 memory files banked.)*

 2 + *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — End of Session 61 (Research Engine Round 1 code shipped across 4 commits; Level 3 Waverly A/B failed verified_quality_gate; root cause documented; flags default OFF pr

 + eserves v1 production behavior; Round 1.1 planning needed before the flags can flip on.)*

 4 ---

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 57 lines

 6 ## CURRENT STATE — update this after each session

 8 + **Session 61 shipped Research Engine Round 1 code across 4 commits. Level 3 Waverly A/B failed the verified_quality gate; Level 3 halted at target 1 per plan's "stop on first failure" rule. Flag

 + s default OFF so production is unchanged — v1 behavior is byte-for-byte preserved.**

 9 +

 10 + ### Session 61 commits (all on main)

 11 +

 12 + | commit | scope |

 13 + |---|---|

 14 + | `978499b` | Part 0: shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision helper in contact_extractor, rewire extract_from_multiple + ResearchJob._merge_contacts). Level 2 Waverly check: 37/37 contacts prese

 + rved, zero regression. 7 unit tests green. |

 15 + | `bd7a562` | Part 1: three feature flags on ResearchJob.__init__ (enable_url_dedup, l15_step5_skip_threshold, log_claude_usage). All default OFF. Module-level Claude usage capture in contact_ex

 + tractor.py is safe across run_in_executor thread boundaries because CPython GIL + serial ResearchQueue guarantee. 20 unit tests green. |

 16 + | `21d9b3b` | Part 2: A/B harness (scripts/ab_research_engine.py + ab_analyze.py). Measurement-grade cost from response.usage token counts. --max-cost-usd ceiling. 3 numerical gates. |

 17 + | `e62f4be` | Round 1.1 patch: URL dedup keeps longest content per URL (first-occurrence rule lost full-page content to short Serper snippets). 21 unit tests green. |

 18 +

 19 + ### Level 3 Waverly A/B — FAIL, stopped at target 1

 20 +

 21 + Two runs against "Waverly School District 145" NE:

 22 +

 23 + | metric | v1 baseline | v2 flags-on (first-wins) | v2 flags-on (longest-wins) |

 24 + |---|---|---|---|

 25 + | contacts total | 34 / 36 | 20 | 23 |

 26 + | contacts verified | **30 / 30** | **19** | **22** |

 27 + | claude calls | 134 / 137 | 65 | 69 |

 28 + | wall clock (s) | 334 / 329 | 171 | 179 |

 29 + | cost usd (measurement) | $0.80 / $0.81 | $0.41 | $0.44 |

 30 + | verified_quality_gate (≥95%) | — | **FAIL** (63%) | **FAIL** (73%) |

 31 + | cost_reduction_gate (≤90%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 32 + | wall_clock_gate (≤105%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 33 +

 34 + **Root cause of verified loss** (13 unique contacts in v1 that v2 missed):

 35 +

 36 + - **URL dedup is lossy for Serper snippets.** Each `_add_raw_from_serper` call appends per-query with different snippet text — the same URL gets 2-4 distinct snippet entries where each snippet h

 + ighlights sentences matching that query. Dedup by URL (either first-wins OR longest-wins) collapses those to one entry and loses the distinct per-query highlighting. Longest-wins recovered ~3 co

 + ntacts vs first-wins, but ~8 contacts remain lost.

 37 + - **L15 Step 5 skip at threshold=15** drops discovery search contacts. Waverly had >15 VERIFIED before L15 step 5, so the flag triggered and skipped 5 Serper discovery queries + 1 Claude extract

 + ion. Estimated 3-5 of the lost 13 contacts came from there.

 38 +

 39 + Both losses are legitimate — neither path can be recovered by a small patch to the existing flags. Round 1 in its current shape is incompatible with the 95% verified quality floor.

 40 +

 41 + **Round 1.1 options (require fresh planning, did NOT ship in S61):**

 42 +

 43 + 1. **Per-URL content merge:** instead of dedup, concatenate all distinct contents for a URL into one Claude call. Preserves all snippet text + full page content with one call instead of N. Requi

 + res careful token budgeting (20k char cap per extract_contacts call). Most promising, biggest scope change.

 44 + 2. **Raise L15 Step 5 threshold to 30+:** at threshold 30, Waverly's 30 verified wouldn't trigger the skip. But Conejo Valley / Cincinnati / Cypress-Fairbanks probably also land at 30-40 verifie

 + d, making the skip almost never fire — savings vanish.

 45 + 3. **Ship only usage logging, abandon URL dedup + L15 skip:** gives measurement-grade cost data for Round 2 planning but zero cost savings in Round 1. Honest minimum.

 46 + 4. **Different cost lever entirely:** batch L9 claude calls (5-10 pages per call instead of 1 per page). Round 2 scope but might be bringable forward.

 47 +

 48 + **Round 1 code state: ship as-is.** The flags default OFF so `agent/main.py`'s 4 call sites still produce byte-for-byte v1 behavior. The module-level usage capture is gated on `log_claude_usage=

 + True` — it's off unless the harness explicitly flips it. Test suite is 21/21 green. No production risk. The flags are available as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 49 +

 50 + **Budget burned in Session 61 A/B runs: ~$4.00** (Level 2 Waverly pre-merge check ~$1.50, A/B run 1 ~$1.21, A/B run 2 ~$1.25). Well under the $5 harness ceiling. Level 3 targets 2-5 (Lake Zurich

 + , Conejo Valley, Cincinnati, Cypress-Fairbanks) NOT executed — stopped on first failure per plan rule, saved ~$8.

 51 +

 52 + ### Session 61 lessons (most recent, load-bearing)

 53 +

 54 + - **`raw_pages` is not a page list — it's an extraction-request list.** Multiple entries per URL are intentional: different Serper queries produce distinct snippet highlighting for the same URL,

 + and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers add full-page versions of the same URL. Any dedup that collapses them to one entry loses information. This wasn't in the Round 1 plan's assumption set

 + because I looked at the dedup target without tracing the population pattern.

 55 + - **"Pure cost win with zero quality loss" is a claim that must be verified against real data, not asserted at plan time.** The Round 1 plan assumed URL dedup was lossless. It wasn't. The A/B ha

 + rness caught this on the very first target — which is exactly what the harness is for — but the plan's estimated impact was wrong by a meaningful margin.

 56 + - **95% is a strict floor, not a soft target.** The plan set verified_quality_gate at `v2 ≥ v1 × 0.95` (CLAUDE.md quality bar). Under that rule, losing 8 of 30 verified is a 27% loss — 5.4x the

 + allowed tolerance. No amount of cost savings compensates for that loss under Steven's "no sacrifice" quality rule.

 57 + - **Stop-on-first-failure is the right rule.** I saved ~$8 and ~60 min by not running Cincinnati / Conejo / Lake Zurich / Cypress-Fairbanks. The failure pattern is mechanical (Serper snippet los

 + s + L15 Step 5 skip), same root cause on every target. More data points would have confirmed the same finding, not revealed new ones.

 58 + - **Level 2 pre-merge checks catch pure regressions; Level 3 A/B checks catch design failures.** Level 2 (stash → run → unstash → diff) proved Part 0 was safe. Level 3 (two engines side-by-side

 + with real targets) proved Part 1 flags were unsafe as designed. Both matter — one is not a substitute for the other.

 59 + - **Ship with flags OFF by default. Failed flags stay dark.** The three Round 1 flags exist in `ResearchJob.__init__` but default to values that preserve v1 behavior. Production is unchanged. Th

 + e next session can iterate on flag behavior without having to revert and re-ship anything.

 60 +

 61 + ---

 62 +

 63 + ## SESSION 60 HISTORY (kept for continuity)

 64 +

 65 **Session 60 ended mid-execution at context limit.** Two distinct pieces of work:

 67 ### 1. Schedule ID map correction — SHIPPED (commit `846aaed`)

 - 978499b Part 0 shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision, 7 tests, Level 2 Waverly check green)

 - bd7a562 Part 1 three feature flags (all default OFF, 20 tests green)

 - 21d9b3b Part 2 A/B harness with real response.usage cost tracking

 - 163fc8f CLAUDE.md session wrap with full root cause + Round 1.1 options

 Level 3 validation stopped at Waverly on verified_quality_gate failure: v1=30 verified, v2=22 (73% of baseline, below the 95% floor). Root cause: URL dedup collapses distinct per-query Serper snippet

 highlighting, and L15 Step 5 skip drops discovery contacts. Both losses are legitimate — not fixable by small patches. Round 1.1 needs fresh planning (per-URL content MERGE, not dedup, is the most promising

 direction).

 Zero production risk. All three flags default OFF; production's 4 ResearchJob call sites continue to get byte-for-byte v1 behavior. Flags remain as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 ❯ ok i dont see a question or instructions. what are you saying what are we doing? whats next?

 ⏺ Sorry — I finished the execution and wrapped up without handing control back cleanly. There's no active question. Here's where we are and the options:

 Where we stand:

 - Round 1 code is shipped on main across 5 commits. Flags default OFF so production (your 4 ResearchJob call sites in agent/main.py ) is byte-for-byte v1 — nothing changed for you.

 - The A/B harness is built and working. It caught a real quality regression on the very first target (Waverly), which is exactly what it's for.

 - Round 1 as designed cannot pass your 95% verified-quality floor. URL dedup loses per-query Serper snippet highlighting; L15 Step 5 skip drops discovery contacts. Both losses are legitimate.

 - ~$4 burned of your $25/week research budget. I stopped after Waverly instead of burning ~$8 more on 4 more targets that would fail the same way.

 Three things we could do next — your call:

 1. Plan Round 1.1 properly. The most promising direction is per-URL content MERGE (concatenate distinct pieces per URL into one Claude call) instead of dedup. Needs a fresh pressure-tested plan before any

 code. ~30-60 min of plan-mode work, no API spend.

 2. Ship only the measurement flag + abandon the cost flags. Keep log_claude_usage (measurement-grade cost instrument for all future rounds), turn off the other two. Zero cost savings in Round 1 but gives Round

 2/3 the data they need. ~20 min, no API spend.

 3. Park research engine, pick up Session 60 carryover instead. The 6 diocesan sequences (IDs 2008-2013) are still sitting waiting for you to activate in the Outreach UI. The 9 pending dioceses + BUG 5

 shared-city gap + F9 scanner redesign are also on the carryover list. Any of those are useful non-research-engine work.

 What do you want to do?

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 57 lines

 6 ## CURRENT STATE — update this after each session

 8 + **Session 61 shipped Research Engine Round 1 code across 4 commits. Level 3 Waverly A/B failed the verified_quality gate; Level 3 halted at target 1 per plan's "stop on first failure" rule. Flag

 + s default OFF so production is unchanged — v1 behavior is byte-for-byte preserved.**

 9 +

 10 + ### Session 61 commits (all on main)

 11 +

 12 + | commit | scope |

 13 + |---|---|

 14 + | `978499b` | Part 0: shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision helper in contact_extractor, rewire extract_from_multiple + ResearchJob._merge_contacts). Level 2 Waverly check: 37/37 contacts prese

 + rved, zero regression. 7 unit tests green. |

 15 + | `bd7a562` | Part 1: three feature flags on ResearchJob.__init__ (enable_url_dedup, l15_step5_skip_threshold, log_claude_usage). All default OFF. Module-level Claude usage capture in contact_ex

 + tractor.py is safe across run_in_executor thread boundaries because CPython GIL + serial ResearchQueue guarantee. 20 unit tests green. |

 16 + | `21d9b3b` | Part 2: A/B harness (scripts/ab_research_engine.py + ab_analyze.py). Measurement-grade cost from response.usage token counts. --max-cost-usd ceiling. 3 numerical gates. |

 17 + | `e62f4be` | Round 1.1 patch: URL dedup keeps longest content per URL (first-occurrence rule lost full-page content to short Serper snippets). 21 unit tests green. |

 18 +

 19 + ### Level 3 Waverly A/B — FAIL, stopped at target 1

 20 +

 21 + Two runs against "Waverly School District 145" NE:

 22 +

 23 + | metric | v1 baseline | v2 flags-on (first-wins) | v2 flags-on (longest-wins) |

 24 + |---|---|---|---|

 25 + | contacts total | 34 / 36 | 20 | 23 |

 26 + | contacts verified | **30 / 30** | **19** | **22** |

 27 + | claude calls | 134 / 137 | 65 | 69 |

 28 + | wall clock (s) | 334 / 329 | 171 | 179 |

 29 + | cost usd (measurement) | $0.80 / $0.81 | $0.41 | $0.44 |

 30 + | verified_quality_gate (≥95%) | — | **FAIL** (63%) | **FAIL** (73%) |

 31 + | cost_reduction_gate (≤90%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 32 + | wall_clock_gate (≤105%) | — | PASS | PASS |

 33 +

 34 + **Root cause of verified loss** (13 unique contacts in v1 that v2 missed):

 35 +

 36 + - **URL dedup is lossy for Serper snippets.** Each `_add_raw_from_serper` call appends per-query with different snippet text — the same URL gets 2-4 distinct snippet entries where each snippet h

 + ighlights sentences matching that query. Dedup by URL (either first-wins OR longest-wins) collapses those to one entry and loses the distinct per-query highlighting. Longest-wins recovered ~3 co

 + ntacts vs first-wins, but ~8 contacts remain lost.

 37 + - **L15 Step 5 skip at threshold=15** drops discovery search contacts. Waverly had >15 VERIFIED before L15 step 5, so the flag triggered and skipped 5 Serper discovery queries + 1 Claude extract

 + ion. Estimated 3-5 of the lost 13 contacts came from there.

 38 +

 39 + Both losses are legitimate — neither path can be recovered by a small patch to the existing flags. Round 1 in its current shape is incompatible with the 95% verified quality floor.

 40 +

 41 + **Round 1.1 options (require fresh planning, did NOT ship in S61):**

 42 +

 43 + 1. **Per-URL content merge:** instead of dedup, concatenate all distinct contents for a URL into one Claude call. Preserves all snippet text + full page content with one call instead of N. Requi

 + res careful token budgeting (20k char cap per extract_contacts call). Most promising, biggest scope change.

 44 + 2. **Raise L15 Step 5 threshold to 30+:** at threshold 30, Waverly's 30 verified wouldn't trigger the skip. But Conejo Valley / Cincinnati / Cypress-Fairbanks probably also land at 30-40 verifie

 + d, making the skip almost never fire — savings vanish.

 45 + 3. **Ship only usage logging, abandon URL dedup + L15 skip:** gives measurement-grade cost data for Round 2 planning but zero cost savings in Round 1. Honest minimum.

 46 + 4. **Different cost lever entirely:** batch L9 claude calls (5-10 pages per call instead of 1 per page). Round 2 scope but might be bringable forward.

 47 +

 48 + **Round 1 code state: ship as-is.** The flags default OFF so `agent/main.py`'s 4 call sites still produce byte-for-byte v1 behavior. The module-level usage capture is gated on `log_claude_usage=

 + True` — it's off unless the harness explicitly flips it. Test suite is 21/21 green. No production risk. The flags are available as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.

 49 +

 50 + **Budget burned in Session 61 A/B runs: ~$4.00** (Level 2 Waverly pre-merge check ~$1.50, A/B run 1 ~$1.21, A/B run 2 ~$1.25). Well under the $5 harness ceiling. Level 3 targets 2-5 (Lake Zurich

 + , Conejo Valley, Cincinnati, Cypress-Fairbanks) NOT executed — stopped on first failure per plan rule, saved ~$8.

 51 +

 52 + ### Session 61 lessons (most recent, load-bearing)

 53 +

 54 + - **`raw_pages` is not a page list — it's an extraction-request list.** Multiple entries per URL are intentional: different Serper queries produce distinct snippet highlighting for the same URL,

 + and direct-scrape / Firecrawl / Exa layers add full-page versions of the same URL. Any dedup that collapses them to one entry loses information. This wasn't in the Round 1 plan's assumption set

 + because I looked at the dedup target without tracing the population pattern.

 55 + - **"Pure cost win with zero quality loss" is a claim that must be verified against real data, not asserted at plan time.** The Round 1 plan assumed URL dedup was lossless. It wasn't. The A/B ha

 + rness caught this on the very first target — which is exactly what the harness is for — but the plan's estimated impact was wrong by a meaningful margin.

 56 + - **95% is a strict floor, not a soft target.** The plan set verified_quality_gate at `v2 ≥ v1 × 0.95` (CLAUDE.md quality bar). Under that rule, losing 8 of 30 verified is a 27% loss — 5.4x the

 + allowed tolerance. No amount of cost savings compensates for that loss under Steven's "no sacrifice" quality rule.

 57 + - **Stop-on-first-failure is the right rule.** I saved ~$8 and ~60 min by not running Cincinnati / Conejo / Lake Zurich / Cypress-Fairbanks. The failure pattern is mechanical (Serper snippet los

 + s + L15 Step 5 skip), same root cause on every target. More data points would have confirmed the same finding, not revealed new ones.

 58 + - **Level 2 pre-merge checks catch pure regressions; Level 3 A/B checks catch design failures.** Level 2 (stash → run → unstash → diff) proved Part 0 was safe. Level 3 (two engines side-by-side

 + with real targets) proved Part 1 flags were unsafe as designed. Both matter — one is not a substitute for the other.

 59 + - **Ship with flags OFF by default. Failed flags stay dark.** The three Round 1 flags exist in `ResearchJob.__init__` but default to values that preserve v1 behavior. Production is unchanged. Th

 + e next session can iterate on flag behavior without having to revert and re-ship anything.

 60 +

 61 + ---

 62 +

 63 + ## SESSION 60 HISTORY (kept for continuity)

 64 +

 65 **Session 60 ended mid-execution at context limit.** Two distinct pieces of work:

 67 ### 1. Schedule ID map correction — SHIPPED (commit `846aaed`)

 - 978499b Part 0 shared dedup fix (upgrade-on-collision, 7 tests, Level 2 Waverly check green)

 - bd7a562 Part 1 three feature flags (all default OFF, 20 tests green)

 - 21d9b3b Part 2 A/B harness with real response.usage cost tracking

 Level 3 validation stopped at Waverly on verified_quality_gate failure: v1=30 verified, v2=22 (73% of baseline, below the 95% floor). Root cause: URL dedup collapses distinct per-query Serper snippet

 highlighting, and L15 Step 5 skip drops discovery contacts. Both losses are legitimate — not fixable by small patches. Round 1.1 needs fresh planning (per-URL content MERGE, not dedup, is the most promising

 direction).

 Zero production risk. All three flags default OFF; production's 4 ResearchJob call sites continue to get byte-for-byte v1 behavior. Flags remain as lab instruments for Round 1.1 experimentation.
