 1491 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 1492 + # PROSPECT WRITE METHODS (Session 61 — promoted from Sessions 38/43 ephemeral scripts)

 1493 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 1494 + #

 1495 + # These four functions commit the POST /prospects + POST /sequenceStates patterns

 1496 + # that Sessions 38 and 43 used as one-shot inline scripts. The library-level

 1497 + # capture satisfies Rule 18 (never re-derive prospect-add code; use the committed

 1498 + # functions) and Rule 17 (every prospect create requires a populated IANA

 1499 + # timezone, enforced by validate_prospect_inputs at the code boundary).

 1500 + #

 1501 + # Design mirrors the S59 sequence hardening pattern:

 1502 + # - validate_prospect_inputs is a standalone, zero-API-call validator

 1503 + # - create_prospect calls it first and refuses to POST on failure

 1504 + # - find_prospect_by_email lets the caller dedup before create

 1505 + # - add_prospect_to_sequence is a thin POST /sequenceStates wrapper

 1506 +

 1507 + # Placeholder/reserved email prefixes that always produce hard bounces.

 1508 + _PLACEHOLDER_EMAIL_PREFIXES = (

 1509 + "[email" , # literal template leaks like "[email protected]"

 1510 + "test@" ,

 1511 + "example@" ,

 1512 + "noreply@" ,

 1513 + "no-reply@" ,

 1514 + "donotreply@" ,

 1515 + "do-not-reply@" ,

 1516 + "postmaster@" ,

 1517 + "mailer-daemon@" ,

 1518 + "abuse@" ,

 1519 + )

 1520 +

 1521 + # Generic free-mail domains that indicate the contact is a personal address

 1522 + # rather than a legitimate work address. Blocked unless explicitly allow-listed

 1523 + # (caller passes allow_generic_email=True for cases where a personal email is

 1524 + # intentional, e.g., a sole-proprietor LLC).

 1525 + _GENERIC_FREE_MAIL_DOMAINS = frozenset ({

 1526 + "gmail.com" , "googlemail.com" ,

 1527 + "yahoo.com" , "yahoo.co.uk" , "ymail.com" ,

 1528 + "hotmail.com" , "hotmail.co.uk" ,

 1529 + "outlook.com" , "live.com" , "msn.com" ,

 1530 + "icloud.com" , "me.com" , "mac.com" ,

 1531 + "aol.com" , "protonmail.com" , "proton.me" ,

 1532 + })

 1533 +

 1534 + # Regex for validating the local-part of an email. Rejects apostrophes

 1535 + # (the O'Brien bug from S61 diocesan drip — apostrophe local-parts technically

 1536 + # parse under RFC 5322 quoted-strings but most SMTP servers reject them on send),

 1537 + # whitespace, and obvious garbage. Intentionally strict.

 1538 + _EMAIL_LOCAL_RE = re. compile ( r"^[A-Za-z0-9._+\-]+$" ) if False else None

 1539 + # Import re lazily to match the existing pattern in validate_sequence_inputs

 1540 + import re as _re # noqa: E402 — placed after helpers to avoid reorder churn

 1541 +

 1542 + _EMAIL_LOCAL_PART_RE = _re. compile ( r"^[A-Za-z0-9._+\-]+$" )

 1543 +

 1544 +

 1545 + def validate_prospect_inputs (

 1546 + first_name: str ,

 1547 + last_name: str ,

 1548 + email: str ,

 1549 + *,

 1550 + title: str = "" ,

 1551 + company: str = "" ,

 1552 + state: str = "" ,

 1553 + timezone: str = "" ,

 1554 + tags: list [ str ] | None = None ,

 1555 + allow_generic_email: bool = False ,

 1556 + ) -> dict :

 1557 + """

 1558 + Pre-write validation for an Outreach prospect. Zero API calls.

 1559 +

 1560 + Returns { "passed" : bool , "failures" : list [ str ], "warnings" : list [ str ]}.

 1561 + Never raises. All failures are collected so the caller can log or display

 1562 + the full list rather than fix-fail-fix-fail.

 1563 +

 1564 + Hard checks (Session 61 Rule 17 — timezone is code-enforced, not process):

 1565 + - first_name non-empty

 1566 + - last_name non-empty

 1567 + - email local@domain shape, no apostrophe in local-part, no whitespace

 1568 + - email prefix not a placeholder ( "[email..." , "test@" , "noreply@" , ...)

 1569 + - email domain not in generic free-mail set (unless allow_generic_email= True )

 1570 + - timezone non-empty AND parses via zoneinfo.ZoneInfo without raising

 1571 + - title/company do not contain automation language (reuses S59 scanner)

 1572 + """

 1573 + from tools.timezone_lookup import is_valid_iana_timezone

 1574 +

 1575 + failures: list [ str ] = []

 1576 + warnings: list [ str ] = []

 1577 +

 1578 + # ── 1. Name fields non-empty ──────────────────────────────────────

 1579 + if not (first_name or "" ).strip():

 1580 + failures.append( "first_name is empty. Every prospect must have a first name." )

 1581 + if not (last_name or "" ).strip():

 1582 + failures.append( "last_name is empty. Every prospect must have a last name." )

 1583 +

 1584 + # ── 2. Email shape + placeholder + domain checks ──────────────────

 1585 + email_clean = (email or "" ).strip().lower()

 1586 + if not email_clean:

 1587 + failures.append( "email is empty. Every prospect must have an email address." )

 1588 + elif email_clean.count( "@" ) != 1 :

 1589 + failures.append( f"email {email!r} does not have exactly one '@'." )

 1590 + else :

 1591 + local, domain = email_clean.split( "@" , 1 )

 1592 +

 1593 + # Placeholder prefix check

 1594 + for prefix in _PLACEHOLDER_EMAIL_PREFIXES:

 1595 + if email_clean.startswith(prefix):

 1596 + failures.append(

 1597 + f"email {email!r} starts with placeholder prefix {prefix!r} . "

 1598 + f"This is not a real address and will hard-bounce. "

 1599 + f"Common source: template leaks like '[email protected]'."

 1600 + )

 1601 + break

 1602 +

 1603 + # Local-part shape (catches O'Brien apostrophe bug + whitespace)

 1604 + if not _EMAIL_LOCAL_PART_RE.match(local):

 1605 + failures.append(

 1606 + f"email local-part {local!r} contains disallowed characters. "

 1607 + f"Allowed: A-Z a-z 0-9 . _ + - . Apostrophes, whitespace, and "

 1608 + f"other special chars cause SMTP-side rejections even if RFC 5322 "

 1609 + f"technically permits them under quoted-string rules."

 1610 + )

 1611 +

 1612 + # Domain shape

 1613 + if "." not in domain or domain.startswith( "." ) or domain.endswith( "." ):

 1614 + failures.append( f"email domain {domain!r} is malformed." )

 1615 +

 1616 + # Generic free-mail block (unless allow-listed)

 1617 + if not allow_generic_email and domain in _GENERIC_FREE_MAIL_DOMAINS:

 1618 + failures.append(

 1619 + f"email domain {domain!r} is a generic free-mail provider. "

 1620 + f"Cold outreach to personal addresses damages sender reputation. "

 1621 + f"Pass allow_generic_email=True only for legitimate sole-proprietor cases."

 1622 + )

 1623 +

 1624 + # ── 3. Timezone REQUIRED + IANA-valid (Rule 17) ───────────────────

 1625 + if not (timezone or "" ).strip():

 1626 + failures.append(

 1627 + "timezone is empty. Rule 17 (Session 61): every Outreach prospect "

 1628 + "create requires a populated IANA timezone string. Derive from state "

 1629 + "via tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone(state). Never fall back."

 1630 + )

 1631 + elif not is_valid_iana_timezone(timezone):

 1632 + failures.append(

 1633 + f"timezone {timezone!r} is not a valid IANA identifier. "

 1634 + f"Must parse via zoneinfo.ZoneInfo(tz) without raising. "

 1635 + f"Examples: 'America/New_York', 'America/Chicago', 'America/Los_Angeles'."

 1636 + )

 1637 +

 1638 + # ── 4. Automation language in title / company ────────────────────

 1639 + for field_name, field_value in [( "title" , title), ( "company" , company)]:

 1640 + if field_value:

 1641 + hits = _scan_banned_phrases(field_value, _BANNED_META_PHRASES)

 1642 + if hits:

 1643 + failures.append(

 1644 + f" {field_name} contains banned automation language {hits!r} . "

 1645 + f"Outreach prospect fields are visible in the UI."

 1646 + )

 1647 +

 1648 + # ── 5. Tag sanity (warning only) ──────────────────────────────────

 1649 + if tags is not None :

 1650 + if not isinstance (tags, list ):

 1651 + failures.append( f"tags must be a list, got { type (tags).__name__} ." )

 1652 + elif not all ( isinstance (t, str ) and t for t in tags):

 1653 + failures.append( "tags must be a list of non-empty strings." )

 1654 +

 1655 + return {

 1656 + "passed" : len (failures) == 0 ,

 1657 + "failures" : failures,

 1658 + "warnings" : warnings,

 1659 + }

 1660 +

 1661 +

 1662 + def create_prospect (

 1663 + first_name: str ,

 1664 + last_name: str ,

 1665 + email: str ,

 1666 + *,

 1667 + title: str = "" ,

 1668 + company: str = "" ,

 1669 + state: str = "" ,

 1670 + timezone: str = "" ,

 1671 + tags: list [ str ] | None = None ,

 1672 + owner_id: int = 11 ,

 1673 + verify_inputs: bool = True ,

 1674 + allow_generic_email: bool = False ,

 1675 + ) -> dict :

 1676 + """

 1677 + Create an Outreach prospect via POST /prospects.

 1678 +

 1679 + Rule 17 + S59 hardening pattern: calls validate_prospect_inputs FIRST and

 1680 + refuses to fire the HTTP request on any validation failure. If validation

 1681 + fails, returns { "error" : ..., "validation_failures" : [...]} with NO API call

 1682 + made. On success, returns { "prospect_id" : str , "email" : str }.

 1683 +

 1684 + Owner defaults to Steven 's user ID (11). Mailbox is NOT set at prospect

 1685 + create time — it 's bound at sequenceState create via add_prospect_to_sequence.

 1686 +

 1687 + Session 38 lesson (feedback_outreach_torecipients.md): this path does NOT

 1688 + touch toRecipients or templates. Only the prospect resource itself.

 1689 + """

 1690 + if verify_inputs:

 1691 + validation = validate_prospect_inputs(

 1692 + first_name=first_name,

 1693 + last_name=last_name,

 1694 + email=email,

 1695 + title=title,

 1696 + company=company,

 1697 + state=state,

 1698 + timezone=timezone,

 1699 + tags=tags,

 1700 + allow_generic_email=allow_generic_email,

 1701 + )

 1702 + if not validation[ "passed" ]:

 1703 + return {

 1704 + "error" : "prospect_validation_failed" ,

 1705 + "validation_failures" : validation[ "failures" ],

 1706 + "email" : email,

 1707 + }

 1708 +

 1709 + email_clean = (email or "" ).strip().lower()

 1710 +

 1711 + attributes: dict = {

 1712 + "firstName" : first_name.strip(),

 1713 + "lastName" : last_name.strip(),

 1714 + "emails" : [email_clean],

 1715 + }

 1716 + if title:

 1717 + attributes[ "title" ] = title.strip()

 1718 + attributes[ "occupation" ] = title.strip()

 1719 + if company:

 1720 + attributes[ "company" ] = company.strip()

 1721 + if state:

 1722 + attributes[ "addressState" ] = state.strip().upper()

 1723 + if timezone:

 1724 + attributes[ "timeZone" ] = timezone.strip()

 1725 + if tags:

 1726 + attributes[ "tags" ] = list (tags)

 1727 +

 1728 + payload = {

 1729 + "data" : {

 1730 + "type" : "prospect" ,

 1731 + "attributes" : attributes,

 1732 + "relationships" : {

 1733 + "owner" : {

 1734 + "data" : { "type" : "user" , "id" : str (owner_id)},

 1735 + },

 1736 + },

 1737 + }

 1738 + }

 1739 +

 1740 + try :

 1741 + response = _api_post( "/prospects" , payload)

 1742 + except Exception as e:

 1743 + return {

 1744 + "error" : f"outreach_api_error: {e} " ,

 1745 + "email" : email_clean,

 1746 + }

 1747 +

 1748 + prospect_id = response.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 1749 + if not prospect_id:

 1750 + return {

 1751 + "error" : "outreach_response_missing_prospect_id" ,

 1752 + "raw" : response,

 1753 + "email" : email_clean,

 1754 + }

 1755 +

 1756 + logger.info( f" created prospect {prospect_id} for {email_clean} " )

 1757 + return {

 1758 + "prospect_id" : prospect_id,

 1759 + "email" : email_clean,

 1760 + }

 1761 +

 1762 +

 1763 + def find_prospect_by_email ( email: str ) -> dict | None :

 1764 + """

 1765 + Look up an existing Outreach prospect by email.

 1766 +

 1767 + Returns a dict with { "prospect_id" , "email" , "owner_id" , "sequence_count" }

 1768 + for the first match, or None if no match.

 1769 +

 1770 + Used by prospect_loader for dedup: before create_prospect, check if the

 1771 + contact already exists in Outreach. If yes, reuse the existing ID and

 1772 + proceed directly to add_prospect_to_sequence.

 1773 + """

 1774 + email_clean = (email or "" ).strip().lower()

 1775 + if not email_clean:

 1776 + return None

 1777 + try :

 1778 + # Outreach v2 API: filter[emails] accepts a single email value

 1779 + result = _api_get( "/prospects" , { "filter[emails]" : email_clean, "page[size]" : "1" })

 1780 + except Exception as e:

 1781 + logger.warning( f" find_prospect_by_email( {email_clean} ) error: {e} " )

 1782 + return None

 1783 +

 1784 + data = result.get( "data" , [])

 1785 + if not data:

 1786 + return None

 1787 +

 1788 + item = data[ 0 ]

 1789 + attrs = item.get( "attributes" , {})

 1790 + owner_rel = (item.get( "relationships" , {}).get( "owner" , {}) or {}).get( "data" ) or {}

 1791 +

 1792 + return {

 1793 + "prospect_id" : item.get( "id" ),

 1794 + "email" : email_clean,

 1795 + "owner_id" : owner_rel.get( "id" ),

 1796 + "first_name" : attrs.get( "firstName" , "" ),

 1797 + "last_name" : attrs.get( "lastName" , "" ),

 1798 + "title" : attrs.get( "title" , "" ),

 1799 + "sequence_count" : attrs.get( "sequenceCount" , 0 ),

 1800 + }

 1801 +

 1802 +

 1803 + def add_prospect_to_sequence (

 1804 + prospect_id: str ,

 1805 + sequence_id: int | str ,

 1806 + *,

 1807 + mailbox_id: int = 11 ,

 1808 + ) -> dict :

 1809 + """

 1810 + Create a sequenceState linking a prospect to a sequence on a mailbox.

 1811 +

 1812 + POST /sequenceStates with the JSON:API relationships payload. Mailbox 11 is

 1813 + Steven 's default (confirmed S61 by reading existing sequenceStates on

 1814 + sequences 1999 , 1939 , 1857 — all use mailbox_id= 11 ).

 1815 +

 1816 + Returns { "sequence_state_id" : str , "prospect_id" : str , "sequence_id" : str }

 1817 + on success, or { "error" : ..., "prospect_id" : str , "sequence_id" : str }

 1818 + on failure.

 1819 +

 1820 + Session 38 lesson (feedback_outreach_sequence_order.md): the target sequence

 1821 + MUST be activated in the Outreach UI before this call. If the sequence is

 1822 + paused, the resulting sequenceState will go in as `paused`, not `active`.

 1823 + This function does NOT verify activation — callers should use

 1824 + prospect_loader.execute_load_plan which includes the pre-flight check.

 1825 + """

 1826 + payload = {

 1827 + "data" : {

 1828 + "type" : "sequenceState" ,

 1829 + "relationships" : {

 1830 + "prospect" : {

 1831 + "data" : { "type" : "prospect" , "id" : str (prospect_id)},

 1832 + },

 1833 + "sequence" : {

 1834 + "data" : { "type" : "sequence" , "id" : str (sequence_id)},

 1835 + },

 1836 + "mailbox" : {

 1837 + "data" : { "type" : "mailbox" , "id" : str (mailbox_id)},

 1838 + },

 1839 + },

 1840 + }

 1841 + }

 1842 +

 1843 + try :

 1844 + response = _api_post( "/sequenceStates" , payload)

 1845 + except Exception as e:

 1846 + return {

 1847 + "error" : f"outreach_api_error: {e} " ,

 1848 + "prospect_id" : str (prospect_id),

 1849 + "sequence_id" : str (sequence_id),

 1850 + }

 1851 +

 1852 + state_id = response.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 1853 + if not state_id:

 1854 + return {

 1855 + "error" : "outreach_response_missing_sequence_state_id" ,

 1856 + "raw" : response,

 1857 + "prospect_id" : str (prospect_id),

 1858 + "sequence_id" : str (sequence_id),

 1859 + }

 1860 +

 1861 + logger.info(

 1862 + f" added prospect {prospect_id} to sequence {sequence_id} "

 1863 + f"(mailbox {mailbox_id} ) -> sequenceState {state_id} "

 1864 + )

 1865 + return {

 1866 + "sequence_state_id" : state_id,

 1867 + "prospect_id" : str (prospect_id),

 1868 + "sequence_id" : str (sequence_id),

 1869 + }

 1870 +

 1871 +

 1872 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 1873 # SEQUENCE EXPORT

 1874 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 159 - # Iterate groups in sorted key order, round-robin across days

 159 + # Assignment: each diocese rotates its OWN contacts through the day list,

 160 + # so within any single day you see a mix of dioceses and within any single

 161 + # diocese the contacts are spread across all days. Day N within a diocese

 162 + # takes contacts [N, N+len(days), N+2*len(days), ...] from that diocese's

 163 + # sorted list. That gives two properties at once:

 164 + # (a) every day touches every diocese that has enough contacts

 165 + # (b) VERIFIED contacts (which sort first) land in earlier days

 167 plans: list [LoadPlan] = []

 179 def test_build_load_plan_round_robin_by_diocese ():

 180 + # 6 contacts (3 per diocese) across 2 days. Each diocese should split

 181 + # 2-1 across the two days (because 3 contacts rotate: 0→Mon, 1→Tue, 2→Mon).

 182 + # The key guarantee: no diocese ends up entirely on one day when it has

 183 + # more contacts than days_count.

 187 sequence_id_for= lambda c: _SEQ_MAP[c.diocese_or_group],

 189 )

 159 - # Iterate groups in sorted key order, round-robin across days

 159 + # Assignment: each diocese rotates its OWN contacts through the day list,

 160 + # so within any single day you see a mix of dioceses and within any single

 161 + # diocese the contacts are spread across all days. Day N within a diocese

 162 + # takes contacts [N, N+len(days), N+2*len(days), ...] from that diocese's

 163 + # sorted list. That gives two properties at once:

 164 + # (a) every day touches every diocese that has enough contacts

 165 + # (b) VERIFIED contacts (which sort first) land in earlier days

 167 plans: list [LoadPlan] = []

 2 *Last updated: 2026-04-13 (Session 61)*

 4 **Purpose:** This file is auto-loaded at session start via a Claude Code `SessionStart` hook. Its job is to prevent "did I build this already?" amnesia by listing every committed Scout capability

 with file:line pointers. If you're about to write code that feels like it might already exist, search this file first.

 6 **Maintenance:** hand-updated when a new module or public function ships. A session that adds to `tools/` must also add an entry here in the same commit. See Rule 18 in CLAUDE.md.

 156 for key in groups:

 159 - # Iterate groups in sorted key order, round-robin across days

 159 + # Assignment: each diocese rotates its OWN contacts through the day list,

 160 + # so within any single day you see a mix of dioceses and within any single

 161 + # diocese the contacts are spread across all days. Day N within a diocese

 162 + # takes contacts [N, N+len(days), N+2*len(days), ...] from that diocese's

 163 + # sorted list. That gives two properties at once:

 164 + # (a) every day touches every diocese that has enough contacts

 165 + # (b) VERIFIED contacts (which sort first) land in earlier days

 166 sorted_group_keys = sorted (groups.keys())

 167 plans: list [LoadPlan] = []

 162 - day_idx = 0

 179 def test_build_load_plan_round_robin_by_diocese ():

 181 + # 2-1 across the two days (because 3 contacts rotate: 0→Mon, 1→Tue, 2→Mon).

 2 *Last updated: 2026-04-13 (Session 61)*

 4 **Purpose:** This file is auto-loaded at session start via a Claude Code `SessionStart` hook. Its job is to prevent "did I build this already?" amnesia by listing every committed Scout capability

 with file:line pointers. If you're about to write code that feels like it might already exist, search this file first.

 159 - # Iterate groups in sorted key order, round-robin across days

 159 + # Assignment: each diocese rotates its OWN contacts through the day list,

 160 + # so within any single day you see a mix of dioceses and within any single

 161 + # diocese the contacts are spread across all days. Day N within a diocese

 162 + # takes contacts [N, N+len(days), N+2*len(days), ...] from that diocese's

 163 + # sorted list. That gives two properties at once:

 164 + # (a) every day touches every diocese that has enough contacts

 165 + # (b) VERIFIED contacts (which sort first) land in earlier days

 166 sorted_group_keys = sorted (groups.keys())

 167 plans: list [LoadPlan] = []

 162 - day_idx = 0

 164 - # Flatten: go group-by-group, pulling one at a time until all empty.

 168 - if not groups[key]:

 170 - contact = groups[key].pop(0)

 172 - tags = tags_for(contact)

 174 contact=contact,

 175 - sequence_id= sid ,

 175 + sequence_id= sequence_id_for(contact) ,

 176 mailbox_id=mailbox_id,

 177 - tags= tags ,

 178 - day_bucket= days[day_idx % len(days)] ,

 177 + tags= tags_for(contact) ,

 178 + day_bucket= day ,

 179 ))

 180 - day_idx += 1

 181 + # Sort the final list by day so Batch 1 / Batch 2 reads cleanly in the

 182 + # state file, then by diocese (for stable diff) and email (for determinism).

 183 + plans.sort(key= lambda p: (p.day_bucket, p.contact.diocese_or_group, p.contact.email.lower()))

 184 +

 185 return plans

 179 def test_build_load_plan_round_robin_by_diocese ():

 324 - Pull 5-10 real rows and aggregate. Label the result explicitly: `measurement / sample / extrapolation / estimate / unknown`.
 325 - If unknown and the number matters, say "unknown — here's how to find out." Do NOT fill in a plausible range. (Session 59 fabricated "$200-800 cost / 30 hours" for 384 research jobs — real num
 bers were $135-365 and 47 hours, both directions wrong.)
 327 + **PREFLIGHT: Prospect add to sequence** — triggers on any task adding prospects to an Outreach sequence, one or many, manual or automated.
 328 + - Load: `feedback_never_manual_outreach_upload.md`, `feedback_outreach_sequence_order.md`, `feedback_outreach_torecipients.md`, `feedback_outreach_intervals.md`, `feedback_timezone_required_bef
 + ore_sequence.md`, `outreach_api_access.md`
 329 + - Grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for `create_prospect` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `validate_prospect_inputs` / `find_prospect_by_email`. If missing, STOP — do not write a new one-shot. Che
 + ck `docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md` and `git log --since=120days` for prior `prospect` + `load` commits first. Promote ephemeral patterns before using them (Rule 18).
 330 + - Verify target sequence is active via the `sequences[id].attributes.enabled` check in `tools/prospect_loader._sequence_is_enabled` before writing.
 331 + - Every contact MUST have a populated IANA timezone derived from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Missing tz = skip the contact, never fall back (Rule 17).

 332 + - Mailbox ID is 11 for all of Steven's sequences (confirmed S61 by reading existing sequenceStates on 1999/1939/1857).

 333 + - Dedup via `find_prospect_by_email` before `create_prospect`.

 334 + - Stagger POSTs: never burst >20 sequenceStates within a 60-second window.

 335 + - Never bypass `validate_prospect_inputs`.

 336 +

 329 - ## CRITICAL RULES (top 15 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 339 + ## CRITICAL RULES (top 17 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 341 1. **Always enter plan mode** before non-trivial builds. New scanners, strategies, schema changes, multi-file refactors — all require `Enter Plan Mode` + Steven's sign-off. Session 51 shipped 7 f

 eatures without plan mode; 3 had BLOCKER bugs. Established Session 52.

 342 2. **Always push code from Claude Code via git**, never `/push_code` in Telegram (4096 char truncation). `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` directly from Claude Code terminal. Hard rule since

 Session 19.

 ⎿   Added 2 lines
 354 14. **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call `execute_tool()` directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_impo
 rt`, `/import_*`, `/enrich_leads`, all `/prospect_*`. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling
 tools.
 355 15. **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement. **ALL sequences are drafts** — always G
 oogle Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval. Never auto-finalize.
 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.
 357 + 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca
 + uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default
 + tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.
 358 + 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI
 + LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 + lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 + already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 360 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 156 for key in groups:

 157 groups[key].sort(key= lambda c: (-c.confidence_rank(), c.email.lower()))

 159 - # Iterate groups in sorted key order, round-robin across days

 159 + # Assignment: each diocese rotates its OWN contacts through the day list,

 160 + # so within any single day you see a mix of dioceses and within any single

 161 + # diocese the contacts are spread across all days. Day N within a diocese

 162 + # takes contacts [N, N+len(days), N+2*len(days), ...] from that diocese's

 163 + # sorted list. That gives two properties at once:

 164 + # (a) every day touches every diocese that has enough contacts

 165 + # (b) VERIFIED contacts (which sort first) land in earlier days

 166 sorted_group_keys = sorted (groups.keys())

 167 plans: list [LoadPlan] = []

 162 - day_idx = 0

 164 - # Flatten: go group-by-group, pulling one at a time until all empty.

 165 - # This produces a diocese-diverse daily slice.

 166 - while any(groups[k] for k in sorted_group_keys):

 167 - for key in sorted_group_keys:

 168 - if not groups[key]:

 169 - continue

 170 - contact = groups[key].pop(0)

 171 - sid = sequence_id_for(contact)

 172 - tags = tags_for(contact)

 169 + for key in sorted_group_keys:

 170 + group_contacts = groups[key]

 171 + for i, contact in enumerate (group_contacts):

 172 + day = days[i % len (days)]

 173 plans.append(LoadPlan(

 174 contact=contact,

 175 - sequence_id= sid ,

 175 + sequence_id= sequence_id_for(contact) ,

 176 mailbox_id=mailbox_id,

 177 - tags= tags ,

 178 - day_bucket= days[day_idx % len(days)] ,

 177 + tags= tags_for(contact) ,

 178 + day_bucket= day ,

 179 ))

 180 - day_idx += 1

 181 + # Sort the final list by day so Batch 1 / Batch 2 reads cleanly in the

 182 + # state file, then by diocese (for stable diff) and email (for determinism).

 183 + plans.sort(key= lambda p: (p.day_bucket, p.contact.diocese_or_group, p.contact.email.lower()))

 184 +

 185 return plans

 324 - Pull 5-10 real rows and aggregate. Label the result explicitly: `measurement / sample / extrapolation / estimate / unknown`.

 325 - If unknown and the number matters, say "unknown — here's how to find out." Do NOT fill in a plausible range. (Session 59 fabricated "$200-800 cost / 30 hours" for 384 research jobs — real num

 bers were $135-365 and 47 hours, both directions wrong.)

 327 + **PREFLIGHT: Prospect add to sequence** — triggers on any task adding prospects to an Outreach sequence, one or many, manual or automated.

 328 + - Load: `feedback_never_manual_outreach_upload.md`, `feedback_outreach_sequence_order.md`, `feedback_outreach_torecipients.md`, `feedback_outreach_intervals.md`, `feedback_timezone_required_bef

 + ore_sequence.md`, `outreach_api_access.md`

 329 + - Grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for `create_prospect` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `validate_prospect_inputs` / `find_prospect_by_email`. If missing, STOP — do not write a new one-shot. Che

 + ck `docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md` and `git log --since=120days` for prior `prospect` + `load` commits first. Promote ephemeral patterns before using them (Rule 18).

 330 + - Verify target sequence is active via the `sequences[id].attributes.enabled` check in `tools/prospect_loader._sequence_is_enabled` before writing.

 331 + - Every contact MUST have a populated IANA timezone derived from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Missing tz = skip the contact, never fall back (Rule 17).

 332 + - Mailbox ID is 11 for all of Steven's sequences (confirmed S61 by reading existing sequenceStates on 1999/1939/1857).

 333 + - Dedup via `find_prospect_by_email` before `create_prospect`.

 334 + - Stagger POSTs: never burst >20 sequenceStates within a 60-second window.

 335 + - Never bypass `validate_prospect_inputs`.

 336 +

 329 - ## CRITICAL RULES (top 15 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 339 + ## CRITICAL RULES (top 17 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 341 1. **Always enter plan mode** before non-trivial builds. New scanners, strategies, schema changes, multi-file refactors — all require `Enter Plan Mode` + Steven's sign-off. Session 51 shipped 7 f

 eatures without plan mode; 3 had BLOCKER bugs. Established Session 52.

 342 2. **Always push code from Claude Code via git**, never `/push_code` in Telegram (4096 char truncation). `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` directly from Claude Code terminal. Hard rule since

 Session 19.

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 2 lines

 354 14. **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call `execute_tool()` directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_impo

 rt`, `/import_*`, `/enrich_leads`, all `/prospect_*`. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling

 tools.

 355 15. **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement. **ALL sequences are drafts** — always G

 oogle Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval. Never auto-finalize.

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 + 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 + uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 + tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 + 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 + LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 + lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 + already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 360 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 324 - Pull 5-10 real rows and aggregate. Label the result explicitly: `measurement / sample / extrapolation / estimate / unknown`.

 325 - If unknown and the number matters, say "unknown — here's how to find out." Do NOT fill in a plausible range. (Session 59 fabricated "$200-800 cost / 30 hours" for 384 research jobs — real num

 bers were $135-365 and 47 hours, both directions wrong.)

 327 + **PREFLIGHT: Prospect add to sequence** — triggers on any task adding prospects to an Outreach sequence, one or many, manual or automated.

 328 + - Load: `feedback_never_manual_outreach_upload.md`, `feedback_outreach_sequence_order.md`, `feedback_outreach_torecipients.md`, `feedback_outreach_intervals.md`, `feedback_timezone_required_bef

 + ore_sequence.md`, `outreach_api_access.md`

 329 + - Grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for `create_prospect` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `validate_prospect_inputs` / `find_prospect_by_email`. If missing, STOP — do not write a new one-shot. Che

 + ck `docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md` and `git log --since=120days` for prior `prospect` + `load` commits first. Promote ephemeral patterns before using them (Rule 18).

 330 + - Verify target sequence is active via the `sequences[id].attributes.enabled` check in `tools/prospect_loader._sequence_is_enabled` before writing.

 331 + - Every contact MUST have a populated IANA timezone derived from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Missing tz = skip the contact, never fall back (Rule 17).

 332 + - Mailbox ID is 11 for all of Steven's sequences (confirmed S61 by reading existing sequenceStates on 1999/1939/1857).

 333 + - Dedup via `find_prospect_by_email` before `create_prospect`.

 334 + - Stagger POSTs: never burst >20 sequenceStates within a 60-second window.

 335 + - Never bypass `validate_prospect_inputs`.

 336 +

 337 ---

 329 - ## CRITICAL RULES (top 15 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 339 + ## CRITICAL RULES (top 17 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 341 1. **Always enter plan mode** before non-trivial builds. New scanners, strategies, schema changes, multi-file refactors — all require `Enter Plan Mode` + Steven's sign-off. Session 51 shipped 7 f

 eatures without plan mode; 3 had BLOCKER bugs. Established Session 52.

 342 2. **Always push code from Claude Code via git**, never `/push_code` in Telegram (4096 char truncation). `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` directly from Claude Code terminal. Hard rule since

 Session 19.

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 2 lines

 354 14. **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call `execute_tool()` directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_impo

 rt`, `/import_*`, `/enrich_leads`, all `/prospect_*`. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling

 tools.

 355 15. **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement. **ALL sequences are drafts** — always G

 oogle Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval. Never auto-finalize.

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 + 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 + uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 + tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 + 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 + LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 + lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 + already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 360 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 324 - Pull 5-10 real rows and aggregate. Label the result explicitly: `measurement / sample / extrapolation / estimate / unknown`.

 325 - If unknown and the number matters, say "unknown — here's how to find out." Do NOT fill in a plausible range. (Session 59 fabricated "$200-800 cost / 30 hours" for 384 research jobs — real num

 bers were $135-365 and 47 hours, both directions wrong.)

 327 + **PREFLIGHT: Prospect add to sequence** — triggers on any task adding prospects to an Outreach sequence, one or many, manual or automated.

 328 + - Load: `feedback_never_manual_outreach_upload.md`, `feedback_outreach_sequence_order.md`, `feedback_outreach_torecipients.md`, `feedback_outreach_intervals.md`, `feedback_timezone_required_bef

 + ore_sequence.md`, `outreach_api_access.md`

 329 + - Grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for `create_prospect` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `validate_prospect_inputs` / `find_prospect_by_email`. If missing, STOP — do not write a new one-shot. Che

 + ck `docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md` and `git log --since=120days` for prior `prospect` + `load` commits first. Promote ephemeral patterns before using them (Rule 18).

 330 + - Verify target sequence is active via the `sequences[id].attributes.enabled` check in `tools/prospect_loader._sequence_is_enabled` before writing.

 331 + - Every contact MUST have a populated IANA timezone derived from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Missing tz = skip the contact, never fall back (Rule 17).

 332 + - Mailbox ID is 11 for all of Steven's sequences (confirmed S61 by reading existing sequenceStates on 1999/1939/1857).

 333 + - Dedup via `find_prospect_by_email` before `create_prospect`.

 334 + - Stagger POSTs: never burst >20 sequenceStates within a 60-second window.

 335 + - Never bypass `validate_prospect_inputs`.

 329 - ## CRITICAL RULES (top 15 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 339 + ## CRITICAL RULES (top 17 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 341 1. **Always enter plan mode** before non-trivial builds. New scanners, strategies, schema changes, multi-file refactors — all require `Enter Plan Mode` + Steven's sign-off. Session 51 shipped 7 f

 eatures without plan mode; 3 had BLOCKER bugs. Established Session 52.

 342 2. **Always push code from Claude Code via git**, never `/push_code` in Telegram (4096 char truncation). `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` directly from Claude Code terminal. Hard rule since

 Session 19.

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 2 lines

 354 14. **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call `execute_tool()` directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_impo

 rt`, `/import_*`, `/enrich_leads`, all `/prospect_*`. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling

 tools.

 355 15. **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement. **ALL sequences are drafts** — always G

 oogle Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval. Never auto-finalize.

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 + 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 + uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 + tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 + 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 + LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 + lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 + already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 360 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 2 *Last updated: 2026-04-13 (Session 61)*

 with file:line pointers. If you're about to write code that feels like it might already exist, search this file first.

 6 **Maintenance:** hand-updated when a new module or public function ships. A session that adds to `tools/` must also add an entry here in the same commit. See Rule 18 in CLAUDE.md.

 324 - Pull 5-10 real rows and aggregate. Label the result explicitly: `measurement / sample / extrapolation / estimate / unknown`.

 325 - If unknown and the number matters, say "unknown — here's how to find out." Do NOT fill in a plausible range. (Session 59 fabricated "$200-800 cost / 30 hours" for 384 research jobs — real num

 bers were $135-365 and 47 hours, both directions wrong.)

 327 + **PREFLIGHT: Prospect add to sequence** — triggers on any task adding prospects to an Outreach sequence, one or many, manual or automated.

 328 + - Load: `feedback_never_manual_outreach_upload.md`, `feedback_outreach_sequence_order.md`, `feedback_outreach_torecipients.md`, `feedback_outreach_intervals.md`, `feedback_timezone_required_bef

 + ore_sequence.md`, `outreach_api_access.md`

 329 + - Grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for `create_prospect` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `validate_prospect_inputs` / `find_prospect_by_email`. If missing, STOP — do not write a new one-shot. Che

 + ck `docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md` and `git log --since=120days` for prior `prospect` + `load` commits first. Promote ephemeral patterns before using them (Rule 18).

 330 + - Verify target sequence is active via the `sequences[id].attributes.enabled` check in `tools/prospect_loader._sequence_is_enabled` before writing.

 331 + - Every contact MUST have a populated IANA timezone derived from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Missing tz = skip the contact, never fall back (Rule 17).

 332 + - Mailbox ID is 11 for all of Steven's sequences (confirmed S61 by reading existing sequenceStates on 1999/1939/1857).

 333 + - Dedup via `find_prospect_by_email` before `create_prospect`.

 334 + - Stagger POSTs: never burst >20 sequenceStates within a 60-second window.

 335 + - Never bypass `validate_prospect_inputs`.

 336 +

 337 ---

 329 - ## CRITICAL RULES (top 15 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 339 + ## CRITICAL RULES (top 17 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 341 1. **Always enter plan mode** before non-trivial builds. New scanners, strategies, schema changes, multi-file refactors — all require `Enter Plan Mode` + Steven's sign-off. Session 51 shipped 7 f

 eatures without plan mode; 3 had BLOCKER bugs. Established Session 52.

 342 2. **Always push code from Claude Code via git**, never `/push_code` in Telegram (4096 char truncation). `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` directly from Claude Code terminal. Hard rule since

 Session 19.

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 2 lines

 354 14. **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call `execute_tool()` directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_impo

 rt`, `/import_*`, `/enrich_leads`, all `/prospect_*`. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling

 tools.

 355 15. **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement. **ALL sequences are drafts** — always G

 oogle Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval. Never auto-finalize.

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 + 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 + uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 + tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 + 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 + LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 + lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 + already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 360 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 359 + 19. **Never show Outreach backend numeric IDs to Steven.** `prospect_id`, `sequence_state_id`, `sequence_id`, `mailbox_id`, `owner_id`, `template_id`, `schedule_id` — all meaningless to him, al

 + l get translated to human names at the presentation boundary. Prospect ID → "First Last (email@domain)". Sequence ID → the sequence name from `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md` / `get_seque

 + nces()` (diocesan 2008-2013 → "Philadelphia/Cincinnati/Detroit/Cleveland/Boston/Chicago diocesan"). Mailbox 11 → "your mailbox". Owner 11 → "you" / "Steven". SequenceState IDs are internal book

 + keeping — omit entirely and say "the add to <sequence name >". Raw IDs stay in function return dicts for downstream API calls, but never leak into chat/stdout/Telegram/summary text. Full rule in

 + `memory/feedback_no_outreach_ids_in_chat.md`. Session 61 lesson.

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 + ore_sequence.md`, `outreach_api_access.md`

 + ck `docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md` and `git log --since=120days` for prior `prospect` + `load` commits first. Promote ephemeral patterns before using them (Rule 18).

 339 + ## CRITICAL RULES (top 17 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 341 1. **Always enter plan mode** before non-trivial builds. New scanners, strategies, schema changes, multi-file refactors — all require `Enter Plan Mode` + Steven's sign-off. Session 51 shipped 7 f

 eatures without plan mode; 3 had BLOCKER bugs. Established Session 52.

 342 2. **Always push code from Claude Code via git**, never `/push_code` in Telegram (4096 char truncation). `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` directly from Claude Code terminal. Hard rule since

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 2 lines

 354 14. **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call `execute_tool()` directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_impo

 rt`, `/import_*`, `/enrich_leads`, all `/prospect_*`. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling

 tools.

 355 15. **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement. **ALL sequences are drafts** — always G

 oogle Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval. Never auto-finalize.

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 + 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 + uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 + tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 + 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 + LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 + lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 + already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 360 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 359 + 19. **Never show Outreach backend numeric IDs to Steven.** `prospect_id`, `sequence_state_id`, `sequence_id`, `mailbox_id`, `owner_id`, `template_id`, `schedule_id` — all meaningless to him, al

 + l get translated to human names at the presentation boundary. Prospect ID → "First Last (email@domain)". Sequence ID → the sequence name from `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md` / `get_seque

 + nces()` (diocesan 2008-2013 → "Philadelphia/Cincinnati/Detroit/Cleveland/Boston/Chicago diocesan"). Mailbox 11 → "your mailbox". Owner 11 → "you" / "Steven". SequenceState IDs are internal book

 + keeping — omit entirely and say "the add to <sequence name >". Raw IDs stay in function return dicts for downstream API calls, but never leak into chat/stdout/Telegram/summary text. Full rule in

 + `memory/feedback_no_outreach_ids_in_chat.md`. Session 61 lesson.

 361 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 339 + ## CRITICAL RULES (top 17 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 341 1. **Always enter plan mode** before non-trivial builds. New scanners, strategies, schema changes, multi-file refactors — all require `Enter Plan Mode` + Steven's sign-off. Session 51 shipped 7 f

 eatures without plan mode; 3 had BLOCKER bugs. Established Session 52.

 342 2. **Always push code from Claude Code via git**, never `/push_code` in Telegram (4096 char truncation). `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` directly from Claude Code terminal. Hard rule since

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 2 lines

 354 14. **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call `execute_tool()` directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_impo

 rt`, `/import_*`, `/enrich_leads`, all `/prospect_*`. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling

 tools.

 355 15. **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement. **ALL sequences are drafts** — always G

 oogle Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval. Never auto-finalize.

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 + 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 + uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 + tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 + 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 + LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 + lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 + already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 ⎿   Added 1 line

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 359 + 19. **Never show Outreach backend numeric IDs to Steven.** `prospect_id`, `sequence_state_id`, `sequence_id`, `mailbox_id`, `owner_id`, `template_id`, `schedule_id` — all meaningless to him, al

 + l get translated to human names at the presentation boundary. Prospect ID → "First Last (email@domain)". Sequence ID → the sequence name from `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md` / `get_seque

 + nces()` (diocesan 2008-2013 → "Philadelphia/Cincinnati/Detroit/Cleveland/Boston/Chicago diocesan"). Mailbox 11 → "your mailbox". Owner 11 → "you" / "Steven". SequenceState IDs are internal book

 + keeping — omit entirely and say "the add to <sequence name >". Raw IDs stay in function return dicts for downstream API calls, but never leak into chat/stdout/Telegram/summary text. Full rule in

 + `memory/feedback_no_outreach_ids_in_chat.md`. Session 61 lesson.

 361 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 ❯ We're approaching context limits. Before I clear, do the following in order:

 1. Save any feedback, preferences, or user context learned this session to memory

 2. Update the project plan file (e.g. SCOUT_PLAN.md):
 - Mark completed items as done
 - Add any new tasks or additions that came up this session
 - Update 'Current Focus' to reflect exactly where we are
 - If we're mid-task, note precisely what was being attempted and what state it's in

 3. Maintain CLAUDE.md:

 - Add any new rules, conventions, or architectural decisions from this session

 - Update 'Current State' with: what's working, what's broken or in-progress, and the exact next step to take

 - Note any uncommitted or in-progress code changes that the next session needs to be aware of

 - Review for content that is no longer actively relevant to ongoing work (e.g. completed features, resolved decisions, old state). If found, move it IN FULL to the project history

 file — do not summarize or lose detail. If everything in CLAUDE.md is still relevant, don't trim anything.

 4. Update the project history file (e.g. SCOUT_HISTORY.md):

 - Append a numbered session entry (e.g. "## Session 14") with: what changed, why, key decisions, and anything archived from CLAUDE.md

 5. Commit and push all changes

 6. Read back CLAUDE.md and the plan file to verify everything is captured correctly. If anything is missing or inaccurate, fix it before proceeding. Then give me:

 - A brief summary of what we accomplished this session

 - The exact prompt to paste in the next session, which should tell Claude to read the plan file and continue from the Current State in CLAUDE.md

 354 14. **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call `execute_tool()` directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_impo

 rt`, `/import_*`, `/enrich_leads`, all `/prospect_*`. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling

 tools.

 355 15. **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement. **ALL sequences are drafts** — always G

 oogle Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval. Never auto-finalize.

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 + 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 + uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 + tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 + 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 + LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 + lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 + already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 63 contacts loaded into the state file , not the 65 from the plan. Why:

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 1 line

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 359 + 19. **Never show Outreach backend numeric IDs to Steven.** `prospect_id`, `sequence_state_id`, `sequence_id`, `mailbox_id`, `owner_id`, `template_id`, `schedule_id` — all meaningless to him, al

 + l get translated to human names at the presentation boundary. Prospect ID → "First Last (email@domain)". Sequence ID → the sequence name from `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md` / `get_seque

 + nces()` (diocesan 2008-2013 → "Philadelphia/Cincinnati/Detroit/Cleveland/Boston/Chicago diocesan"). Mailbox 11 → "your mailbox". Owner 11 → "you" / "Steven". SequenceState IDs are internal book

 + keeping — omit entirely and say "the add to <sequence name >". Raw IDs stay in function return dicts for downstream API calls, but never leak into chat/stdout/Telegram/summary text. Full rule in

 + `memory/feedback_no_outreach_ids_in_chat.md`. Session 61 lesson.

 361 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 ❯ We're approaching context limits. Before I clear, do the following in order:

 1. Save any feedback, preferences, or user context learned this session to memory

 2. Update the project plan file (e.g. SCOUT_PLAN.md):

 - Mark completed items as done

 - Add any new tasks or additions that came up this session

 - Update 'Current Focus' to reflect exactly where we are

 - If we're mid-task, note precisely what was being attempted and what state it's in

 3. Maintain CLAUDE.md:

 - Add any new rules, conventions, or architectural decisions from this session

 - Update 'Current State' with: what's working, what's broken or in-progress, and the exact next step to take

 - Note any uncommitted or in-progress code changes that the next session needs to be aware of

 - Review for content that is no longer actively relevant to ongoing work (e.g. completed features, resolved decisions, old state). If found, move it IN FULL to the project history

 file — do not summarize or lose detail. If everything in CLAUDE.md is still relevant, don't trim anything.

 4. Update the project history file (e.g. SCOUT_HISTORY.md):

 - Append a numbered session entry (e.g. "## Session 14") with: what changed, why, key decisions, and anything archived from CLAUDE.md

 5. Commit and push all changes

 6. Read back CLAUDE.md and the plan file to verify everything is captured correctly. If anything is missing or inaccurate, fix it before proceeding. Then give me:

 - A brief summary of what we accomplished this session

 - The exact prompt to paste in the next session, which should tell Claude to read the plan file and continue from the Current State in CLAUDE.md

 ⎿   Added 1 line

 356 16. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 357 17. **Timezone is a hard requirement on every Outreach prospect create.** `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs` enforces it at the code boundary — missing or invalid IANA timezone ca

 uses the validator to return `passed=False` and `create_prospect` refuses to fire the HTTP request. Derive from state via `tools.timezone_lookup.state_to_timezone`. Never fall back to a default

 tz; skip the contact and fix the source data. Session 61 diocesan drip lesson.

 358 18. **Never write a new one-shot Outreach prospect loader.** Before writing any prospect-add code, (a) grep `tools/outreach_client.py` for the target function name, (b) check `docs/SCOUT_CAPABI

 LITIES.md`, (c) grep `git log --since=120days` for `prospect` + `load` + `sequence` commits. If the work has been done before but isn't in library code, promote the pattern to `tools/outreach_c

 lient.py` + `tools/prospect_loader.py` BEFORE writing a new one-shot. Ephemeral inline Python scripts (S38 CUE loader, S43 C4 1,119-prospect loader) are the root cause of the "did I build this

 already?" amnesia Steven called out in Sessions 59 and 61. The canonical path is `create_prospect` / `find_prospect_by_email` / `add_prospect_to_sequence` / `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`.

 359 + 19. **Never show Outreach backend numeric IDs to Steven.** `prospect_id`, `sequence_state_id`, `sequence_id`, `mailbox_id`, `owner_id`, `template_id`, `schedule_id` — all meaningless to him, al

 + l get translated to human names at the presentation boundary. Prospect ID → "First Last (email@domain)". Sequence ID → the sequence name from `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md` / `get_seque

 + nces()` (diocesan 2008-2013 → "Philadelphia/Cincinnati/Detroit/Cleveland/Boston/Chicago diocesan"). Mailbox 11 → "your mailbox". Owner 11 → "you" / "Steven". SequenceState IDs are internal book

 + keeping — omit entirely and say "the add to <sequence name >". Raw IDs stay in function return dicts for downstream API calls, but never leak into chat/stdout/Telegram/summary text. Full rule in

 + `memory/feedback_no_outreach_ids_in_chat.md`. Session 61 lesson.

 361 > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 58 - **Level 2 pre-merge checks catch pure regressions; Level 3 A/B checks catch design failures.** Level 2 (stash → run → unstash → diff) proved Part 0 was safe. Level 3 (two engines side-by-side
 with real targets) proved Part 1 flags were unsafe as designed. Both matter — one is not a substitute for the other.
 59 - **Ship with flags OFF by default. Failed flags stay dark.** The three Round 1 flags exist in `ResearchJob.__init__` but default to values that preserve v1 behavior. Production is unchanged. T
 he next session can iterate on flag behavior without having to revert and re-ship anything.

 61 + ### Session 61 second half — diocesan drip library + amnesia root-cause fix
 63 - ## SESSION 60 HISTORY (kept for continuity)
 63 + After parking the research engine, Steven asked me to add the diocesan prospects to sequences 2008-2013 (which he'd already activated) with day + time stagger. My first draft plan framed it as
 + "net new capability" — that framing was WRONG and Steven caught it. Sessions 38 and 43 had already done POST /prospects + POST /sequenceStates work, but always as ephemeral inline Python that n

 + ever got committed, so every subsequent session I opened `tools/outreach_client.py`, saw no `create_prospect` function, and concluded the capability didn't exist. Same amnesia had hit Session 5

 + 9 earlier. The corrected plan (`~/.claude/plans/rosy-jumping-teacup.md` rev 2) treats the amnesia itself as Problem B and fixes it structurally alongside the drip.

 65 + **5 commits on main this half of the session:**

 68 + |---|---|

 69 + | `fdf6920` | Promote prospect-add pattern to library: 4 new functions in `tools/outreach_client.py` (`validate_prospect_inputs`, `create_prospect`, `find_prospect_by_email`, `add_prospect_to_s

 + equence`) + new `tools/timezone_lookup.py` (50 states → IANA) + new `tools/prospect_loader.py` (reusable bulk loader with resumable state file + jittered sleep + pre-flight sequence-enabled che

 + ck + Contact/LoadPlan dataclasses + `build_load_plan` round-robin) + new `scripts/diocesan_drip.py` thin CLI (5 subcommands: `--assign`, `--dry-run`, `--canary`, `--canary-cleanup`, `--execute`

 + , `--verify`) + new `scripts/test_diocesan_drip.py` (15 unit tests). 29 unit tests green total. |

 70 + | `fcd1417` | Amnesia root-cause fix. New `docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md` (~360 line inventory of every committed Scout capability with file:line pointers, organized by module) + new CLAUDE.md PRE

 + FLIGHT: Prospect add to sequence block + Rule 17 (timezone required via `validate_prospect_inputs` code boundary) + Rule 18 (no ephemeral prospect scripts — grep library and git log before writ

 + ing new one-shots). New memory file `feedback_timezone_required_before_sequence.md`. |

 71 + | `fb941a1` | Rule 19 (never show Outreach backend IDs in chat). Memory file `feedback_no_outreach_ids_in_chat.md` with the full translation table (prospect_id → name+email, sequence_id → dioce

 + san name, mailbox_id → "your mailbox", sequenceState IDs → omit entirely). |

 + ion context exactly like the Vercel plugin's knowledge-update banner. **Next session will automatically see the capabilities inventory before Claude proposes any work.**

 85 + **Canary verification — PASSED.** A throwaway "Scout Canary" prospect at `scout-canary-<ts>@codecombat.test` (IANA-reserved `.test` TLD → unresolvable, no real mail delivered) was created and a

 + dded to the Chicago diocesan sequence. Steven screenshot-confirmed it landed on Step 1 (Auto email) with the right sequencer, sending in 6h. Cleaned up: sequenceState DELETE succeeded cleanly (

 + 204). Prospect DELETE returned 403 — `prospects.delete` scope not granted. Steven then manually deleted the orphaned prospect from Outreach UI.

 86 +

 87 + **Session 61 second-half lessons (load-bearing):**

 88 +

 89 + - **The ephemeral-script pattern IS the amnesia.** Sessions 38 and 43 each built `POST /prospects` + `POST /sequenceStates` inline in a heredoc or /tmp/ script that ran once and vanished. Every

 + subsequent session I open `tools/outreach_client.py`, see no `create_prospect` function, and conclude the capability doesn't exist. Rule 18 now requires grepping library code AND `git log --si

 + nce=120days` AND `docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md` before writing any new one-shot prospect-add code. The fix is structural: `docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md` + SessionStart hook force the inventory into

 + every session's context.

 90 + - **CLAUDE.md trim moves institutional memory out of the auto-loaded window.** Session 53 extracted `docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md`, Session 58 extracted `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`. Both files are load-be

 + aring but NOT auto-loaded — CLAUDE.md just says "read by section or grep by keyword" with no forcing function. The SessionStart hook fix is the forcing function for the NEW capabilities index.

 + If you want the FULL SCOUT_RULES.md / SCOUT_REFERENCE.md content in auto-loaded context too, that's a trivial extension of the hook — just add them to the `cat` command.

 91 + - **Code enforcement beats process rules wherever possible.** The timezone rule (Rule 17) is enforced by `validate_prospect_inputs` refusing to fire on empty/invalid IANA — NOT by "remember to

 + populate the field." Same meta-principle as `feedback_code_enforcement_beats_process_rules.md`. The prospect-add amnesia fix is a hybrid: structural (library code + capability inventory + Sessi

 + onStart hook) for what can be automated, process rule (Rule 18) for the judgment call before writing new code.

 92 + - **Never show Outreach backend numeric IDs to Steven.** He reads `prospect_id=669325, sequence 2013, mailbox 11` as pure noise — meaningless strings that make the assistant feel like a log ter

 + minal. Rule 19 requires translating at the presentation boundary: prospect_id → "First Last (email@domain)", sequence_id → diocesan name, mailbox → "your mailbox", sequenceState → omit entirely

 + . Raw IDs stay in function return dicts for downstream API calls but never leak into chat/stdout/Telegram summaries. Full translation table in `memory/feedback_no_outreach_ids_in_chat.md`.

 93 + - **`raw_pages` in research_engine is an extraction-REQUEST list, not a page list.** Multiple entries per URL are intentional: Serper adds per-query snippets with different highlighting, direct

 + scrape / Firecrawl / Exa add full-page markdown. Any URL-based dedup loses information. Round 1 URL dedup flag is lossy and stays OFF. `memory/feedback_raw_pages_is_extraction_requests.md`.

 110 + ```bash

 111 + cd /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout

 114 +

 115 + This runs Tuesday's batch (17 contacts) with 5-15 min jittered sleeps between POSTs. Total wall clock ~2.5-3 hours. The script reads `data/diocesan_drip_state.json`, picks up the day bucket for

 + today's date via `datetime.now(ZoneInfo('America/Chicago')).date()`, filters to pending contacts, verifies each target sequence is still enabled, dedups via `find_prospect_by_email`, creates p

 + rospects, adds them to sequences, updates state file atomically after every POST, writes one line per contact to `data/diocesan_drip_audit.jsonl`.

 116 +

 117 + If Steven wants Monday's batch caught up (it was skipped because he called EOD on Apr 13 night), add `--force-day 2026-04-13` and run once for Monday before running the Tuesday batch. Or run tw

 + ice back-to-back. Both are resumable via the state file.

 119 + After Tuesday's batch completes, `scripts/diocesan_drip.py --verify` will read the state file and compare against live Outreach sequenceStates for all 6 sequences.

 120 +

 121 + Wednesday's Session 63 and Thursday's Session 64 each run `--execute` once more. After Thursday, run `--verify` for the final audit.
